title: Virtual Growth Marketing Specialist. Philippines time zone. 250503
description:
Are you seeking a full-time, long-term career that offers exceptional growth potential through advancement opportunities, along with a stable income and bonus incentives? Join our team if you have a keen eye for detail, an unwavering drive for productivity, and a strong sense of responsibility. If you possess self-motivation, assertiveness, and a strong work ethic, coupled with a positive outlook and the ability to excel in a fast-paced work environment, we invite you to reach out to us.



This is for candidates who are keen to develop a professional career that allows you to work remotely and independently. There are career paths with promising future opportunities that have traditionally been accessible only through conventional means. However, our embrace of technology, good jobs are no longer restricted to traditional ways.



About Us

The business has collaborated with a highly acclaimed loan brokerage and independent financial advisory firm, along with several other partners, all of which are fully licensed by government authorities. We offer a diversified suite of financial services and solutions with a personalised approach.



With these partners, we have one of the largest, most-respected, and fastest-growing networks of advisers in the country. We provide sound and comprehensive financial advice at both individual and corporate levels.



The Role

We are seeking a creative, proactive, and detail-oriented Virtual Growth Marketing Specialist based in the Philippines to manage and grow our online presence. You will be supporting a professional focused on helping individuals and businesses with personal wealth accumulation, protection (insurance), and legacy planning. This is a fantastic opportunity for someone passionate about social media who is eager to learn about the financial services and insurance industry.



Key Responsibilities

• Develop, create, and schedule engaging content across various social media platforms (e.g.,
, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok.

• Utilize AI tools effectively for brainstorming content ideas, generating initial drafts, and streamlining workflows.

• Design visually appealing graphics, infographics, and other media assets using tools like Canva, adhering to brand guidelines.

• Create and edit short-form videos (e.g., Reels, TikToks, Shorts) using video editing applications (experience with DaVinci Resolve is a plus) and AI video tools.

• Manage day-to-day activities on social media profiles, including posting content and monitoring engagement.

• Respond to comments, messages, and inquiries in a timely and professional manner, fostering community interaction.

• Actively learn and apply knowledge about personal finance, wealth management, insurance (personal & corporate), and legacy planning to ensure content is relevant, accurate (within compliance guidelines you'll be trained on), and resonates with the target audience.

• Stay updated on the latest social media trends, tools, and best practices.

• Track and report on key social media metrics.

• Collaborate on social media strategies and campaigns.



Qualifications

• Proven experience as a Social Media Manager, Virtual Assistant with social media focus, or similar role.

• Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal.

• Proficiency in using major social media platforms and familiarity with social media management/scheduling tools.

• Strong graphic design skills with demonstrable experience using Canva or similar tools.

• Experience in video creation and editing (familiarity with DaVinci Resolve is highly desirable; experience with other tools like CapCut is also valuable).

• Demonstrable ability to use AI tools for content creation, ideation, or editing.

• Crucial: A strong willingness, aptitude, and enthusiasm for learning about personal finance, insurance, and wealth management concepts. You don't need to be an expert now, but you must be eager to learn!

• Highly organised, reliable, and able to manage tasks independently.

• Access to a stable internet connection and a suitable remote work setup.

• Previous experience working within the financial services or insurance industry is a plus, but not required.]



Application

To apply for the job:

Click the below link to apply, and please follow the instructions.



There will be an assessment and a Microsoft Teams interview video call required, if you are shortlisted.

title: Social Media & Content VA
description:
We are looking for a strategic and organised Social Media & Content VA to own our content output across Instagram,
, TikTok, and LinkedIn. Branded graphics and visual assets are provided to you, you focus on strategy, captions, scheduling, engagement, community management, and blogs.



This is not a design role. We need someone who understands audience, voice, and engagement, someone who can take a finished asset and know exactly how to position it, caption it, and get it in front of the right people.



This is a 2-day per week part-time role to start with genuine potential to grow. You will work directly with the business owner.



What You Will Be Doing

Social Media — All Platforms (Primary Focus)

• Receive branded graphics and video assets weekly from our in-house designer and schedule across Instagram,
, TikTok, Google My business and LinkedIn - we use Metricool - you will need to adjust content to reflect the relevant channel e.g. cant have link in bio for

• Write captions, hooks, and calls-to-action that match our brand voice, professional, approachable, and clear

• Maintain a content calendar planned at least 2 weeks ahead

• Research relevant trends, audio, and hashtags to maximise organic reach

• Monitor and respond to comments and DMs to drive genuine engagement

• Provide a simple fortnightly performance report, reach, engagement, follower growth, and what is and isn’t working



Use Manychat to set up DM workflows for increased engagement



 Group Management

• Actively manage and engage the 
munity group, not just post into it

• Welcome new members, prompt discussion, and keep the group alive and on-brand

• Repurpose content from our asset library into group-appropriate posts and conversation starters

• Monitor and moderate to maintain a positive, professional environment



Blog Content (Fortnightly)

• Work within an existing GPT-assisted blog workflow, brief is provided, you refine and finalise the draft

• Ensure posts are well-structured, on-brand, and ready for business owner review before publishing

• Basic SEO awareness, headings, keywords, and readability



What We Need From You

Please read this carefully. We will ask about these during the hiring process.



Non-negotiable requirements

• Minimum 2–3 years managing social media for a brand, not just personal accounts

• Proven experience across Instagram,
, TikTok, Google My Business and LinkedIn

• Strong written English and the ability to write in a brand voice that is not your own

• Strategic thinker, you understand what makes content perform, not just what makes it look nice

• Exceptional attention to detail, wrong links, typos, and off-brand content are not acceptable

• Proactive, you suggest ideas and spot opportunities without being told

• Available during Australian business hours (at least partial overlap)



Highly regarded

• Experience managing a 
munity group or membership

• Familiarity with Meta Business Suite and TikTok Business Center

• Experience supporting a personal brand, educator, or service-based business

• Blog or content writing experience

• Experience with ClickUp or similar project management tools



What Good Looks Like in This Role

• You treat the
 group like a real community, not a content dump

• You understand the difference between posting content and building an audience

• You can look at a branded graphic or b-roll clip and know instinctively how to position it

• You flag content ideas, trends, and opportunities proactively

• Every piece of content is self-checked before it reaches us

• You meet deadlines without being chased and communicate when something is blocked



What We Offer

• Stable, long-term part-time role with room to grow

• Branded asset library provided, graphics and video assets ready to use each week

• Direct access to the business owner, clear briefs and fast feedback

• A focused content role where your strategic thinking is genuinely valued





How to Apply

We read every application carefully. Generic applications will not be considered. Please send us the following:



• A short intro (3–5 sentences): who you are, what brands you have managed social media for, and what platform you are strongest on.

• Your social media experience: platforms managed, content types, posting frequency, and a result you are proud of.

• A link or example: of social media content you have managed, a profile, a post, or a portfolio. Anonymise if needed.

• One content idea: suggest one piece of content you would create for a legal services brand targeting small business owners. Just one or two sentences, we want to see how you think.

• Start your application with the words “Content Ready” so we know you have read this in full.



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.

title: Video Editor (PHP 358/HR - USD $6/HR | PART Time)
description:
Hey!



We are looking for a Video Editor ROCKSTAR who will turn our long form content into high-performing short form clips and weekly YouTube videos.



We are a marketing and education company that works with English speakers and already have a team of high-performing individuals from the Philippines. Mastery of the English language is a requirement and will be tested prior to hiring.



BENEFITS INCLUDE:

- 100% remote

- Weekly pay

- USD $6 / Hour = 350 PHP / Hour

- Paid training

- No work during the weekends

- Opportunity to go from part time to full time

- We pay for the AI and creative tools you will use (Adobe, Descript, Opus Clip, Canva Pro)

- Grow with a team that values initiative, speed, and high standards



WHAT YOU WILL DO:



Short form (main focus):

- Take long form content (webinars, trainings, YouTube videos, podcasts) and turn it into short form clips for Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and LinkedIn

- Add attention-grabbing hooks, captions, emojis, B-roll, overlays, and pattern interrupts

- Create short testimonial videos from coaching calls, client interviews, and other raw footage

- Organize and manage video assets so clips are easy to find and reuse



Long form & channel support:

- Edit 1 piece of long form content per week (highlight key 
nts, add B-roll, overlays, and graphics)

- Publish that long form video on YouTube (upload, title, description, tags, end screens, cards)

- Create and upload custom YouTube thumbnails that drive clicks

- Handle one-off editing requests as needed (simple promos, clips, intros/outros, etc.)

- Analyze performance of published videos in YouTube Studio (CTR, views, watch time, retention) and provide simple reports and recommendations



Collaboration & cadence:

- Meet once a week with the owner of the company for feedback, direction, and priorities

- Join 1 monthly content planning session with the team

- Use AI tools (that we pay for) to speed up editing, transcription, captioning, and repurposing while still polishing by hand



Expected volume (part-time ~20 hours per week):

- 20–30 finished short form clips per week (30–90 seconds each)

- 1 edited and published long form YouTube video per week

- Ongoing testimonial edits and one-off requests as needed



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR:



Experience & portfolio:

- Proven experience editing short form content from long form videos

- Strong portfolio showing:

Before/after edits from long form to short form

YouTube videos you have edited and thumbnails you have designed

Short testimonial / social proof videos (a plus)



Tool competence (we provide licenses):

You will work daily inside:

- Adobe Premiere Pro (primary editor)

- Basic Adobe After Effects (simple animations, lower thirds, transitions)

- Descript (transcription, rough cuts, repurposing)

- Opus Clip (AI clipping & short-form ideas)

- Canva Pro (YouTube thumbnails & simple graphics)

- YouTube Studio (publishing & analytics)

- Google Drive, Google Docs/Sheets (file management, tracking)

- ClickUp (task management)

- Slack (communication)



You don’t need to be a master of every tool on day one, but you must already be strong in at least one pro editor (Premiere / Final Cut / DaVinci) and be able to learn the rest quickly.

Having experience with video editing is a requirement to be considered for this role.

We will ask you what your favorite dog breed is, answer chowchow in your application so we know you have read the full job posting.

We will start with relatively limited hours (around 20 hours per week to begin) with the opportunity for it to grow as our relationship develops.



REQUIREMENTS:

- Mastery of the English language (spoken and written)

- Strong project management skills and ability to hit volume targets consistently

- Comfortable reviewing analytics and making basic performance recommendations

- Quiet environment to work



TECH REQUIREMENTS (MINIMUM):

- Computer: Windows 10/11 PC or Mac (released within the last 4–5 years)

CPU: Intel i7 / Ryzen 7 / Apple M1 or better (or equivalent)

RAM: Minimum 16 GB (32 GB strongly preferred)

Storage: At least 512 GB SSD, with 150+ GB free for active projects

GPU: Dedicated graphics card with 4 GB VRAM or more (e.g., NVIDIA GTX 1660 / RTX series or equivalent). For Mac, M1/M2 with integrated GPU is fine.



- Software capability:

Must be able to run Adobe Premiere Pro / After Effects smoothly while having multiple Chrome tabs, cloud drives, and AI tools (Descript / Opus Clip / etc.) open.



-High Speed Internet: Stable enough for uploading/downloading large video files and live calls without interruption

Reliable backup option (mobile data or secondary ISP) is a plus



- Other:

Quality headphones and microphone for edit reviews and team calls

Quiet, dedicated workspace suitable for focused editing work



If your current setup struggles with Adobe Premiere or AI tools like Descript / Opus Clip, please upgrade first before applying. This role requires fast, reliable hardware to hit our weekly volume targets.



SCHEDULE:

You must be available to work 4 hours per day, following this schedule: 3 PM – 7 PM Philippines time.



You must APPLY with this link:

title: Application Specialist
description:
**PLEASE CAREFULLY READ ALL THE DETAILS BEFORE APPLYING***



Job Title: Application Specialist

Reports To: Director of Enrollment

Work Type: Full-time, Remote/WFH

Working Hours: Tuesday – Saturday, 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM AZ time

Start Date: ASAP



Role Type: Individual Contributor (high?volume outreach + registration execution)



Role Purpose: 



The Application Specialist is responsible for guiding prospective students through the application and registration process with clarity, accuracy, and urgency. This role executes high?volume outbound and inbound outreach, follows the approved script and required registration fields, and ensures each student interaction is documented correctly, so applications move forward without errors, delays, or rework. The Application Specialist plays a critical role in meeting daily enrollment goals while delivering a clear, supportive student experience that avoids overwhelming.



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES: 



Student Outreach & Registration Execution

Conduct outbound and inbound communication with prospective students via calls, texts, and
. 

Maintain a steady call flow that supports a smooth ~15?minute registration conversation. 

Follow the approved script and required questions on every interaction. 

Guide students through registration completion accurately and efficiently.

Confirm understanding of next steps before ending each interaction. 



Daily Productivity & Pacing 

Meet or exceed daily outreach and registration expectations.

Maintain consistent effort throughout the day rather than batching late?day activity. 

Balance speed with accuracy to prevent rework, errors, or student confusion.

Stay focused on priority outreach queues as assigned.



Quality Control & Compliance 

Ensure every registration includes required and accurate information

Physical address required (PO Box allowed only as mailing address where permitted).

Proof of residency requirements met or properly flagged. 

Correct handling of McKinney?Vento exceptions when applicable. 

Complete duplicate checks prior to registration submission. 

Create correct cases with proper subject line usage. 

Document clear, complete notes including outcomes and next steps. 

Immediately escalate compliance concerns or unclear situations.



Documentation & Systems Accuracy 

Document all calls, texts,
s, and outcomes in the system by the end of the interaction. 

Ensure notes are accurate, concise, and actionable. 

Maintain clean statuses and follow?up indicators. 

Correct errors promptly when identified. 

Clear, compliant documentation with no missing required fields.



Communication & Team Alignment 

Acknowledge updates, changes, or instructions posted by leadership. 

Ask clarifying questions early when unsure rather than guessing.

Collaborate respectfully with peers and Team Leads to maintain workflow consistency.



Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) 

Daily Production Minimums 

85 calls per day, maintaining 80% of current metrics 

3–5 registrations per day maintaining 80% of minimum current metrics 

Maintain 100% script adherence and quality documentation on every contact. 

Quality & Efficiency Indicators based on Dialpad 90% 

Ensure accurate registration completion 

Minimal >5% duplicate registrations or case errors. 

95$ Hour follow?up on lead completion.



Authority & Escalation Guidelines:



Application Specialist Can: 

Execute outreach and registrations independently within established scripts and processes. 

Clarify minor student questions using approved guidance. 

Flag system or process issues promptly. 

Escalate to Team Lead or Manager: 

Unclear eligibility or compliance scenarios. 

Parent or student complaints requiring leadership involvement. 

System issues blocking registration completion. 

Repeated student confusion or process breakdowns.



Strengths Needed for the Role 

Calm, professional communication in a high?volume environment. 

Strong attention to detail and process discipline. 

Ability to maintain pace while delivering supportive student experience.

Accountability for both activity and outcomes. 

Comfort receiving coaching and adjusting quickly.



What “Great” Looks Like  

Registrations are completed accurately the first time. 

Students feel informed, supported, and not overwhelmed. 

Daily call and registration goals are met consistently. 

Minimal rework due to missing information or errors. 

The Applications team operates smoothly with predictable, steady performance.



Required Experience:

Experience in sales or marketing, preferably within an education-related account

Phone sales experience is highly preferred

Proficient in using Salesforce to manage daily tasks, including calls, notes, and follow-ups

title: Senior Graphic Designer (Motion Potential a Plus) — Creative Studio (AU-based, fully remote)
description:
Senior Graphic Designer (Motion Potential a Plus) — Creative Studio (AU-based, fully remote)

Job Title: Senior Graphic Designer — with motion potential Hours: Full-time, 40 hrs/week (open to part-time, 25–30 hrs/week, for the right person) Schedule: Australian business hours overlap required (approx. 7am–4pm Manila / 9am–6pm AEST). At least 5–6 hours of daily overlap with AEST is essential — you'll be reviewing work directly with our onshore creative directors. Type: Long-term, direct hire. We want someone who stays for years and grows with us. Salary: PHP 120,000–220,000/month (USD $2,150–$3,950), depending on experience and the strength of your portfolio. Top of range available for proven senior designers with strong type, system thinking, AI-augmented output, and demonstrable motion ability or potential. Paid via Wise or Payoneer, every 2 weeks. Plus 13th month, paid leave, performance bonuses.



Important: This is a senior creative role. We are not hiring junior designers, Canva-only generalists, or template adapters. If your portfolio doesn't show original brand systems, considered typography, and real layout sensibility, this role isn't right for you yet — and that's okay.



A note on motion: Your primary craft is graphic design — brand, identity, layout, campaign. But part of this role's growth path is motion. We don't expect you to be a senior motion designer on day one. We do expect you to either already have basic motion ability (After Effects, Lottie, simple animation) or be hungry to grow into it fast — and to show us proof you can. We'll invest in your growth here. AI-driven motion tools (Runway, Veo, Sora, Kling, Cavalry) are part of how we'll get there.







About Us

We are Nice Wolf, a creative studio based in Australia. We have three businesses under one roof:



Nice Wolf Studio — brand, campaign, content, and design work for clients who want their work to actually land.

Human Terrain — film and photo production for cinematic, considered creative.

Journey Maker (Digital Locals) — an AI-powered concierge product for hotels and their guests.



Our team has decades of award-winning experience working on global brands. Our onshore creative directors are world-class — they've shipped work you've seen, for clients you'd recognise. We're looking for one senior designer to work directly alongside them, taking projects from raw idea to finished system across brand and campaign work.



We move fast, we hold a high bar, and we use modern AI tooling aggressively to scale our output without dropping quality. We're not interested in designers who are scared of AI, and we're not interested in designers who think AI replaces craft. We want someone who uses both.





The Role

You will be the senior graphic design hand at the studio — embedded with our onshore creative director, leading execution across:



Brand systems — building from the ground up. Logos, marks, type systems, colour, grid systems, and the rules that hold them together.

Campaign and content design — print, digital, OOH, social — with consistency across the full system.

Editorial and layout — long-form documents, decks, brochures, lookbooks, pitch documents.

Design system thinking — building scalable component libraries, brand kits, and templates that let the whole team move faster without going off-brand.

Motion (now or growing into it) — bringing identities and campaigns to life through animation. AI-driven motion tools are part of how we scale this; we'll support you in growing this side of your craft.

AI-augmented production — using the latest generative tools to scale ideation, mood, and output. We'll teach you our stack; you'll bring your own.



You will work directly with our creative director on the toughest, most ambitious work, and own end-to-end execution on more straightforward projects yourself. You'll occasionally jump into Canva to crank out variants or templates for the broader team — not because that's the level of the role, but because senior designers in small studios stay close to the production floor.



This is not a Canva designer role. But you should be happy to work in Canva when the job calls for it. The same way a senior chef will still slice an onion when the kitchen needs it.





What You'll Do

Design brand systems from scratch — identity, type, colour, grid, application, guidelines.

Develop visual identities into full campaigns across print, digital, social, and editorial.

Lead typography and layout decisions across long-form documents, decks, and brochures.

Build and maintain design systems, component libraries, and brand kits in Figma and Canva so the whole studio benefits from your structure.

Animate — at whatever level you're at today, with the support to grow. Whether that's logo reveals, simple identity loops, social cuts, or more ambitious motion work, you'll be encouraged to push your motion craft. AI-driven tools (Runway, Veo, Sora, Kling, Cavalry, Lottie) will be part of your kit.

Use AI tools (Midjourney, Firefly, Krea, Topaz, Claude, Runway, Veo, etc.) to scale your output — generating moodboards, references, base assets, motion clips, and ideation faster than humanly possible alone. We expect you to push these tools further than most people do.

Collaborate directly with our creative director and the broader team. Take direction. Push back when you disagree. Bring your own opinion.

Review work from junior creators and AI-generated output. You're a quality gate.

Help shape how we work — propose new tools, new workflows, new ways to scale output without dropping the bar.





Required Skills (Must Have)

You must have all of these. Please do not apply if you do not.



A senior-level portfolio. We need to see real, original brand systems, real type and layout sensibility, and considered campaign work. If your folio is mostly Canva templates, social tiles, and minor adaptations, this is not the role for you yet.

Strong fundamentals. Typography. Composition. Colour. Hierarchy. The non-negotiables. We can tell within 30 seconds of looking at your portfolio whether you have these.

Brand identity experience. You have built brands from the ground up — not just laid out other people's brands. We want to see the marks, the systems, and the thinking behind them.

Strong typographic sensibility. Pairing, scale, hierarchy, kerning. You sweat the details. You know when type is set well and when it isn't.

Layout and editorial chops. You can take a long-form document, a deck, or a campaign system and make it sing. Grid, rhythm, hierarchy.

Figma fluency. Components, variants, auto layout, design tokens. You build organised files that other people can pick up.

Canva willingness. You don't have to love it, but you have to be willing to use it. Our broader team works in Canva, and you'll occasionally produce templates, decks, and variants there.

AI tool fluency. You actively use generative AI tools in your workflow — Midjourney, Firefly, Krea, Topaz, or similar. You can show us how you've used them on real projects, not just experimentally.

Design system thinking. You don't just make one-off assets — you build the system that lets the next ten assets ship faster.

A creative opinion. You are not a robot. You have a point of view. You can defend it, refine it, or change it when you're convinced — but you have one. Designers without opinions don't survive here.

Comfort with feedback and collaboration. You'll work directly with senior creative directors. You should welcome sharp feedback, give sharp feedback in return, and push the work forward without ego.

Strong English, written and spoken. You'll be reviewing work on Zoom with creative directors and occasionally clients.

Motion Track — Required at Some Level

You need to land somewhere on this spectrum. The further along you are, the higher in the salary band you'll sit:



Already capable: You have shipped real motion work — logo animations, social cuts, identity systems with motion, explainers, or short branded animations. After Effects fluent. Bonus for Cavalry, Cinema 4D, Blender, or Lottie.

Capable basics + hungry: You can do basic motion (simple After Effects, animated logos, Lottie exports, or Canva/Figma motion) and you've actively been growing this side of your craft. You can show us proof — even small experiments, AI-driven motion tests, or self-initiated practice.

Strong fundamentals + serious intent: You don't have shipped motion work yet, but you've been experimenting with AI motion tools (Runway, Veo, Kling, Sora) or learning After Effects on your own. You have a clear plan to grow this skill and you can demonstrate self-direction. This option is the bottom of the salary band — you'll grow into the higher tiers as your motion work develops.



If motion holds zero interest for you, this isn't the right role.

Nice to Have (Bonus Points)

Strong existing motion graphics experience (After Effects, Cavalry, Cinema 4D, Blender, Lottie).

3D and CGI work (Cinema 4D, Blender, Octane, Redshift).

Editorial and print design experience (real print, not just print mockups).

Type design or custom letterform experience.

Experience working remotely with international (especially AU, US, or UK) creative teams.

Hospitality, travel, or luxury brand experience (helpful for Journey Maker).

Experience working with creative agencies or in-house brand teams at a senior level.

Experience training or mentoring other designers.

Experience designing for film, photo, or content production (titles, posters, supers, lower thirds).





Our Tech Stack

These are the tools we work with daily. Strong existing experience with the design tools is essentially required at this level. Experience with the operational tools is a plus; we can teach you those.



Design (you should be strong in most of these):



Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, Photoshop, InDesign)

Figma (advanced — components, variants, design tokens)

Canva (for fast adaptations and team templates)



Motion (current ability or active learning required):



After Effects (basic to advanced — depending on where you sit on the motion spectrum above)

Lottie / Bodymovin for web-friendly motion

Cavalry, Cinema 4D, or Blender — bonus

AI-driven motion tools (Runway, Veo, Sora, Kling) — willingness to use these is essential



AI tooling (we expect fluency in some, willingness to learn the rest):



Midjourney, Firefly, Krea

Runway, Veo, Sora, Kling (for motion and video)

Topaz (upscaling and enhancement)

Claude / ChatGPT (for ideation, writing, and AI-assisted production)



Studio operations (you'll pick these up):



Notion (our entire operating system)

Slack, Google Workspace, StoryChief





Who You Are

You are a senior brand and graphic designer with real range. You move from logo to layout to campaign without losing the thread.

You have taste. You can articulate why something works, not just feel it.

You have opinions. You'll tell us when an idea isn't strong enough. You'll defend your own work and revise it when the argument is good.

You are systems-minded. You build tools and templates that make the next person's work easier.

You are fast and ambitious with AI. You see it as leverage, not as a threat. You'll push our AI workflows further than they are today.

You are calm and collaborative. You work well with senior creatives. You don't need to be the loudest voice in the room to have impact.

You are proud of your craft. You sweat the kerning. You notice the rounded corner that doesn't match the system. You care.

Who You Are NOT

To save us both time, please do not apply if:



Your portfolio is mostly Canva templates, basic social tiles, or copies of other people's brand work.

You are a junior or mid-weight designer hoping this will be a stretch role. It's not.

You have zero interest in motion. Even if you're not strong at it today, you need to want to grow there.

You are uncomfortable with AI tools, or you think they're "cheating."

You can't speak confidently in English on a Zoom call.

You consider yourself a creative director rather than a hands-on designer. This is a hands-on senior role.

You can't take feedback without taking it personally.





How to Apply

We will only consider applications that follow these instructions exactly. This is your first test of attention to detail — and senior designers, of all people, should have it.



Send your application with the following, in this order:



1. Subject line: Senior Graphic Designer — [Your Full Name]



2. A short note (under 200 words). Why does this role interest you? What about Nice Wolf, Human Terrain, or Journey Maker caught your eye? Be specific. We will not read generic AI-generated cover letters — and we can spot them instantly.



3. Your portfolio. This is the most important part of your application. Send a link (Behance, Dribbble, personal site, Notion, or a PDF — we don't care about the format, only the work).



Inside your portfolio, please make sure we can see:



At least one full brand identity you built from the ground up (logo, type, colour, applications)

At least one editorial or campaign piece that shows your layout and typographic sensibility

One example of design system or template work — Figma library, brand kit, component system, or similar

One motion piece or motion experiment — at any level. If you have shipped motion work, show us. If you're learning, show us a self-initiated test, an AI-driven motion piece, or even a 5-second logo animation. We want to see where you are today.

One example of AI-augmented work — show us where AI fitted into your process and what it produced



4. Your CV or LinkedIn URL.



5. A 60–90 second Loom video introducing yourself. Tell us:



Your name and where you are based

The most ambitious creative project you've worked on and what your role was

One AI tool you use in your work and how you use it

Where you sit on the motion spectrum today, and where you want to be in 12 months

One creative opinion you hold strongly (we want to see if you have one)



Do not script it. We want to see how you think on your feet.



6. The word "DEN" somewhere in your application. This is how we know you read this job ad properly.



7. Your expected monthly salary in PHP and your earliest start date.





What Happens Next

We review every application that follows the instructions above. Applications that skip steps will not be reviewed.

Shortlisted candidates get a paid design test (4–8 hours, paid at your full rate). The brief will be a real-world creative challenge — brand fragment, layout exercise, or campaign system.

Strong candidates from the test will be invited to a 45-minute video interview with the founder and one of our creative directors.

Final decision within 2–3 weeks of application.



We are looking for one excellent person. Take your time with your application — we will take ours reading it.



Apply via OnlineJobs.ph. We do not respond to messages on other platforms.

title: Junior Designer/Illustrator
description:
Junior Designer, Remote Hourly

Premium US Skincare Brand



We are hiring a junior designer for a premium direct-to-consumer skincare brand. We need someone who can create clean, modern, premium visuals across product labels, website assets,
 graphics, and marketing materials.



This role is remote and async-friendly. New graduates are welcome. Portfolio quality matters more than years of experience.



Rate

Starting at $5/hr USD 

Performance review after 90 days, with potential rate increase for strong work, reliability, and speed.



Hours

10-20 hours per week to start. 

Initial work will focus mainly on product packaging/labels and
 or website graphics. The scope may expand over time based on performance and business needs.



Pacific timezone overlap is appreciated, but not required.



Responsibilities

• Product packaging and label design in Adobe Illustrator

• Website graphics and assets in Figma

•
 design assets

• Marketing materials and social media graphics

• Light brand and visual system support



Required Skills

• Strong Adobe Illustrator skills

• Strong Figma skills

• Experience using AI design tools such as Adobe Firefly, Midjourney, or ChatGPT

• Must be able to show specific examples of work created or improved using AI tools

• Strong typography sense

• Clean, modern, premium design aesthetic

• Solid written English communication



Preferred Experience

• E-commerce, beauty, skincare, wellness, or premium consumer brand design

• Label or packaging design samples



Paid Trial Project

Selected applicants will complete a small paid trial project before being hired. The trial will be approximately 1-2 hours and paid as a fixed project, typically $10-$20 USD, depending on the task.



How to Apply

Please send your application to: 

Include:

• Your portfolio link

• 2-3 examples of premium-looking design work you are proud of

• At least one example of work where you used AI tools such as Firefly, Midjourney, or ChatGPT. Briefly explain how you used the tool.

• Any product label or packaging samples made in Adobe Illustrator

• Your availability and typical response time

• A short note about why your design style fits a premium skincare brand

• Applications without a portfolio or an AI tool example will not be considered.

Applications are reviewed within 7-10 days. Shortlisted applicants will hear back. We are unable to respond to all submissions.



Note:

Brand Confidentiality

The public post does not disclose the brand name. Shortlisted applicants will receive more details before the paid trial. An NDA may be required before receiving confidential brand assets, product details, or internal briefs.



Ownership / Work Product

All work product created for this role is owned by the brand upon delivery and payment. The designer assigns all rights to brand assets, designs, source files, and deliverables created for the brand.

title: Pinterest & Social Media Marketing Specialist (Etsy Growth)
description:

our Etsy shop through Pinterest, Instagram, and

for someone who understands visual marketing, organic social growth, and driving traffic to 

product listings.

performance.

 Content Support (Required)

 posts that align with brand content

 content planning and promotional posts

 to Etsy listings  

Content Creation & Design (Required)

Qualifications

 content management experience

 engagement and referral traffic

title: Social media and marketing VA
description:
About the company



Oliva Dorado is a premium direct-to-consumer extra virgin olive oil brand. We source single-estate, certified organic oil from a small family farm in Zaragoza, Spain and ship it to health-conscious consumers across the United States. We care deeply about what goes into people's bodies and we are building a brand that earns real trust. We are a small, founder-led team and every person we bring on plays a meaningful role.



Who we are looking for:



We are looking for a driven, all-around VA who genuinely wants to grow inside a brand and learn every part of how a modern consumer business operates. You are not a task-taker waiting for assignments. You take ownership, ask questions, and bring ideas. You want to be part of building something.



Your primary strength is social media and content creation, but you are excited to help wherever the business needs you. You are organized, communicative, and you follow through.



What you will work on:



Social media content creation: Creating posts, carousels, Reels, and Stories for Instagram and TikTok. Both organic brand content and paid ad creative (static and video). Writing captions that match our brand voice.



Video editing: Editing short-form video for Reels, TikTok, and Meta ads. Taking raw footage and turning it into polished, on-brand content. Adding text overlays, transitions, music, and subtitles.



Paid ad support: Helping with Meta ad creative: resizing assets, formatting copy for ads, organizing creative libraries, and keeping track of what is live and what is in testing.



General operations and admin: Whatever else the business needs. This could be research, outreach, scheduling, updating docs, coordinating with partners, or supporting product launches. You are a contributor, not just a specialist.



Must haves:

- Strong portfolio of social media content: posts, carousels, and short-form video

- Comfortable using CapCut, Adobe Premiere, or similar video editing tools

- Comfortable with Canva or similar design tools for static graphics

- Excellent written English: clear, clean, and free of filler

- Reliable internet, reliable computer, and reliable hours

- A genuine desire to grow and learn inside a small brand, not just complete tasks



Big plus if you have:

- Experience in the CPG, food, wellness, or health consumer space

- Experience supporting a direct-to-consumer brand

- Familiarity with Meta Ads Manager or any paid social platform

- Interest in food quality, clean ingredients, or Mediterranean lifestyle content

- Experience with Shopify, Klaviyo, or e-commerce operations in any form



How to apply: Send a short message telling us who you are, what you have worked on, and why Oliva Dorado interests you. Include links to content you have created: social accounts, a portfolio, or specific posts. Applications without work samples will not be considered. We are a small team and we read everything carefully.

title: Experienced Real Estate Media VA | QC & Admin Specialist
description:
Experienced Real Estate Media | QC & Admin Specialist

Department: Quality Control & Delivery / Project Coordination / Customer Support



Work Hours: Monday–Saturday, 7:00 AM–1:30 PM MST



Salary: $8/hour fully trained, with opportunities for performance bonuses and long-term growth within the company. During training, pay ranges from $5 to $7/hour.



Location: Work from Home

Type: Full-Time / Long-Term

Company: SOLD Media Group



About SOLD Media Group

SOLD Media Group is a family-run real estate media company based in Arizona, USA. We create photo, video, drone, and 3D tour content for real estate agents, helping them showcase their listings and sell faster.



We pride ourselves on:

Fast Turnaround Times – Next morning delivery.

Friendly, Reliable Communication – Clients and team members always feel supported.

High-Quality Work – Photos, videos, and virtual tours that get listings SOLD.



We’re a growing real estate media company looking for someone smart, organized, proactive, and driven to grow with us long term. This role is more than basic admin support. We need someone who can become a key part of our operations and help keep the business running smoothly every day.



Your main responsibilities will include quality control for our real estate photo and video projects (reviewing edits, making touch-ups, and ensuring everything meets our standards), delivering completed media to clients, assisting with project management, handling client communication and text follow-ups, creating and scheduling social media content, and supporting other day-to-day business tasks as needed.



We’re looking for someone detail-oriented, dependable, quick to learn, and confident in taking ownership. If you’re the type of person who solves problems, communicates well, and wants to grow into a leadership role, this could be a great fit. As we expand and hire more team members, we want this person to have the opportunity to step into a Virtual Assistant Team Lead position.



What You’ll Be Doing-



Quality Control & Media Delivery:



Review all photos, videos, and 3D tours for quality, consistency, and brand standards

Perform minor edits during QC (color correction, object removal, grass greening, sky replacements)

Deliver all completed media to clients before 10 AM MST

Manage client retouch requests; handle simple edits and escalate complex revisions to the editing team



Project Management & Coordination:



Assist with day-to-day project management to keep all shoots, edits, and deliveries on track

Schedule and manage bookings, timelines, and task tracking within Spiro

Coordinate with photographers, editors, and team members to ensure smooth workflow

Monitor project progress and proactively flag delays or issues



Client Communication & Support:



Handle client communication across text,
, phone, and social media

Send booking confirmations, follow-ups, and post-delivery messages

Answer client questions, provide updates, and ensure a high-quality client experience

Maintain organized records of client interactions and project details



Operations & Administrative Support:



Track daily tasks, priorities, and progress to ensure deadlines are consistently met

Maintain organized systems and documentation across projects

Complete end-of-day Slack check-ins with completed work, next steps, and any issues

Support additional business tasks and process improvements as the company grows



What We’re Looking For: Essential Skills



Strong English (written and spoken)



Attention to detail and ability to manage multiple tasks



Confident communicating with clients via phone,
, and social media



Photo editing skills: Lightroom, AutoHDR, photoshop, Canva (basic retouching)



Google Workspace: Gmail, Drive, Docs, Sheets



Reliable high-speed internet (100 Mb/s+ Upload Internet Connection)



Positive attitude, fast learner, and proactive



Prior experience in real estate media/editing or similar creative production



Leadership or team coordination experience (future Lead VA potential)



Customer service or admin support experience



Arizona Time Availability (MST, UTC-7)

All of our clients operate in Arizona time. Being available during core hours is required.



Why You’ll Love Working With Us:



Supportive Team Culture – Friendly, helpful environment where your growth is valued.



Room to Grow – Opportunities to advance into Lead VA / Team Manager roles.



Creative Work – Hands-on involvement with stunning real estate photos, videos, and 3D tours.



Direct Access to Founders – Work directly with Brendan and Tyler and learn the business from the ground up. We’re looking for an exceptional teammate we can invest in long term, someone we can support and watch grow both personally and professionally at SOLD Media Group.



We value love, happiness, positivity, and having fun in everything we do. We want someone who will thrive in that environment, make a real impact, and grow alongside us as the company continues to expand.



How to Apply:



Please
 
 with the subject line:

Application – VA Admin & QC Specialist: [Your Name]



Include the following:



1. Your updated resume (PDF format)



2. A screenshot of your internet speed test (with proof of date)



3. A 1–2 minute video answering these four questions:



#1 Tell us about yourself — what do you love?



#2 Describe your past work experience and how it relates to this job at SOLD Media Group.



#3 Why do you believe you’d be a great fit for this position?



#4 Do you have any leadership or management experience?



We can’t wait to learn more about you! Please make sure to complete all three steps above so your application is considered.

title: Full-Time Short-Form Video Editor & Creative Strategist (TikTok & Reels Ads)
description:
No Beginners — Only Highly Skilled Editors Apply



We are hiring a full-time, high-level video editor + creative strategist who understands how to create viral short-form content for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and ads.



This role is for someone who can edit at a high level AND think like a marketer — someone who knows how to turn content into high-performing advertorial videos that drive sales.



What We’re Looking For



• Advanced skills in Adobe Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve  

• Strong understanding of viral content, hooks, retention, and pacing  

• Ability to create high-converting short-form videos (Reels, TikTok, insta ads)  

• Skilled with captions, sound effects, transitions, and storytelling  

• Strong marketing mindset — not just editing, but creating content that sells  

• Fast, efficient, and consistent with daily output  

• Reliable and looking for a long-term role  



This is not a beginner position. No part-time. No hobby editors.



Salary & Growth



Starting: $1,500 – $2,500/month  

• Based on skill, creativity, and performance  

• Performance bonuses for high-performing videos  

• Fast growth potential — income increases as performance improves  

• Long-term opportunity with high earning potential  



 Responsibilities



Video Editing  

• Edit short-form videos (10–60+ seconds) for TikTok & Instagram  

• Create advertorial-style content for a streetwear brand  

• Add captions, sound effects, transitions, and pacing  

• Turn raw footage into high-retention content  

• Maintain consistent daily output  



Creative Strategy  

• Help develop video ideas, hooks, and concepts  

• Understand how to market clothing products through content  

• Suggest improvements to increase engagement and conversions  

• Be creative with limited footage (B-roll, alternative visuals, etc.)  



Collaboration  

• Work closely with our team daily  

• Bring your own ideas and creativity  

• Turn concepts into viral-ready content  



Ideal Candidate



• Advanced editor (Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve)  

• Deep understanding of short-form content and virality  

• Strong marketing and storytelling skills  

• Fast, consistent, and detail-oriented  

• Good communication and open to feedback  

• Looking for a long-term, full-time role  



 To Apply



Please send:  

1. Your best short-form video examples (TikTok/Reels/Ads)  

2. A short message explaining your experience with viral content  

3. Your expected salary  

4. Confirmation you are available full-time long-term  

5. Your daily availability  



Final Note



We are looking for someone who wants to grow long-term with a brand.  

If you know how to create content that performs and converts, we want to work with you.

title: Medical Biller
description:

Strong attention to detail and organization

Ability to follow up consistently and communicate professionally



Ideal Candidate:

Someone who is proactive, organized, and comfortable owning the billing process from claim submission through payment.

title: Remote Property Manager
description:
Job Overview

We are seeking a highly responsive, detail-oriented Property Manager to oversee day-to-day operations of residential properties across the United States.

This role requires a strong ownership mindset, excellent communication skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities across tenant relations, maintenance coordination, leasing, compliance, and financial tracking.

This is a hands-on role where responsiveness is critical—you will be expected to actively monitor tasks, follow up consistently, and support both tenants and ownership in real time.



Core Responsibilities

1. Tenant Relations & Communication

Serve as the primary point of contact for tenants

Respond to tenant inquiries via phone, text, and
 promptly

Handle escalations, disputes, and lease violations professionally

Send notices (entry, rent increases, lease renewals, violations)

Coordinate move-ins and move-outs

Maintain strong relationships with tenants and caseworkers



2. Leasing & Lead Management

Manage all incoming leads and inquiries

Schedule and conduct property showings

Convert leads into qualified applicants using effective sales techniques

Screen tenants and provide daily updates on new applications

Prepare lease documents and manage onboarding process

Track lease renewals and proactively follow up (90-day / 60-day notices)





3. Maintenance & Vendor Coordination

Manage maintenance requests and monitor portals daily

Assign and reassign tasks to ensure timely completion

Follow up with vendors and confirm scheduling

Verify completed work with tenants after closure

Coordinate move-in ready turns and repairs

Track open maintenance requests and claims

Schedule preventative maintenance (HVAC, seasonal, etc.)

Manage vendor relationships and accountability



4. Compliance, Inspections & Legal

Coordinate housing authority inspections (HAP compliance)

Ensure properties meet required standards

Schedule and track routine and certification inspections

Serve legal notices (7-day notices, demands, etc.) when required

Prepare documentation for court and eviction processes

Monitor lease compliance and enforce terms



5. Financial Management & Reporting

Track rent collection and monitor delinquencies

Apply and enforce late fees (after 5 days)

Manage and monitor payment plans with tenants

Review and explain P&L statements and owner reports

Reconcile expenses and assign costs to correct properties

Review credit card transactions and categorize expenses

Monitor utilities (water, gas, electric) and provide monthly summaries

Handle mortgage, HOA, and insurance tracking

Reconcile payments with county and vendor portals



6. Owner Communication & Reporting

Maintain strong relationships with property owners

Provide regular updates on property performance

Share monthly reports, maintenance updates, and financial summaries

Obtain approvals for repairs and major expenses

Deliver proactive recommendations to improve performance



7. Administrative & Operational Tasks

Maintain accurate records in CRM/property management systems

Upload invoices and manage documentation

Track lease data, tenant records, and compliance requirements

Monitor insurance policies and renewals

Ensure all utilities and accounts are properly set up

Maintain inventory tracking (filters, maintenance items, etc.)



8. Emergency & After-Hours Support

Act as point of contact for urgent maintenance or safety issues

Coordinate emergency vendor response when needed



Monthly / Recurring Responsibilities

Lease renewal tracking and follow-ups

Delinquency outreach and collection updates

Owner check-ins and reporting

Property condition and maintenance reports

Preventative maintenance planning

Eviction tracking and execution support

Utility and expense reconciliation

Claims tracking (e.g., Nicor Home Solutions)



Qualifications

Prior experience in property management, leasing, or real estate operations

Experience with US-based properties strongly preferred

Strong communication skills (clear, neutral English—written and spoken)

Comfortable handling phone calls with tenants, vendors, and owners

Highly organized with strong attention to detail

Ability to multitask and work independently

Strong problem-solving and follow-up skills

Experience with property management software preferred

Familiarity with Zillow, HotPads, and leasing platforms



Preferred Experience

Section 8 / Housing Authority experience

Vendor coordination and maintenance dispatch

Financial tracking (P&L, expense categorization)

Tenant screening and leasing conversions

Customer service or call-based roles



Required Mindset

Strong ownership mentality (takes responsibility, follows through)

Highly responsive and proactive

Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment

Ability to push tasks forward without constant direction



Application Requirements

Please include the following in your application:

Years of property management experience

Number of units currently or previously managed

Types of properties managed (single-family, multifamily, etc.)

Software used (Doorloop)

Your role (owner, manager, assistant, etc.)

Markets you’ve managed properties in

What parts of your process are automated



Key Traits for Success

Strong communication + follow-up

Detail-oriented and organized

Sales mindset (for leasing conversions)

Accountability and reliability

title: Social Media VA – Real Estate Content Execution
description:
I’m a real estate agent based in Rapid City, SD looking for a skilled Social Media VA to help execute consistent, elevated content across Instagram,
, LinkedIn, and YouTube Shorts.



How this works: I provide the direction, raw video / images, and concepts. You execute.

I use Coffee and Contracts, Canva, edits and ManyChat. The personal side of my content stays with me. What I need is someone who can take what I hand them and turn it into polished, scroll-stopping content that doesn’t look or sound like every other agent’s page.



I’m particular. My content needs to feel clean, elevated, and uniquely mine and personal. Not templated, not AI sounding, not something that could be dropped onto another agent’s account.



I will give feedback and expect revisions. If that works for you, keep reading.



Responsibilities

-Batch create content in Canva including carousels, graphics, reel covers, and post designs using my brand fonts and colors

-Customize Coffee and Contracts content to fit my brand, not used as-is

-Write captions that sound like me. Conversational and natural

-Use AI tools to refine tone, not replace it

-Manage ManyChat including comment triggers and engagement hooks using my existing guides

-Load all content into Rella for review before  and I will schedule content

-Repurpose content across platforms once I build up my content base

-Elevate listing content for pre-market, just listed, and active listings with strong hooks and clean execution



Requirements

-Advanced Canva skills. You can build from scratch, not just edit templates

-Real estate social media experience preferred

-Strong understanding of hooks and what performs

-Experience with ManyChat or ability to learn quickly

-Strong writing skills that sound human

-Efficient. Not looking for someone who spends hours on one reel

-Able to take feedback and revise quickly without pushback

-Must have some overlap with US working hours for communication



Things to Know

-I post all content

-You load content into Rella. I review and schedule

-I post my own listings and manage my grid

-Canva and Coffee and Contracts are already set up





To apply, include

-Examples of reels and carousels (real estate preferred)

-How long it takes you to produce a reel and a carousel

-Your pricing

-Tools and platforms you use

-Why you’re a good fit for a brand that is detail-focused



Open to a 1 month trail period in order to get into a flow of what I am wanting and how I want my brand to look. 



Include the word “Black Hills” at the top of your application so I know you read this.

You know the roadmap, share our goals, and make decisions that move us forward. You're a tastemaker who can spot AI slop instantly - and won't tolerate it.

title: Experienced Brand Manager Assistant to support 12+ Amazon brands for growth, execution, drive performance and operation
description:
Role Overview



We're hiring for a Brand Manager Assistant who will work directly under the Senior Brand Manager to execute day-to-day brand operations. This is a hands-on role focused on the “legwork” that drives performance: optimizing listings, managing keyword research, coordinating creative projects, and pulling actionable data.



If the Senior Brand Manager sets the playbook, the Associate Brand Manager runs the plays — ensuring everything gets executed on time, correctly, and at a high level.



Key Responsibilities

1. Listing Management & Optimization

Create, update, and fully optimize Amazon product listings (titles, bullets, descriptions, images, videos, A+ content).

Conduct keyword research and competitor analysis to improve rankings and visibility.

Ensure all listings remain Rufus and Cosmos compliant with Amazon’s latest requirements.

Test content variations (A/B testing) to improve CTR and conversions.



2. Data Research & Analysis

Pull performance data from Amazon Seller Central and external tools (Data Dive, Helium 10, Jungle Scout).

Identify trends, keyword shifts, and competitor activity.

Provide actionable recommendations to the Senior Brand Manager to improve conversions, CTR, and sales velocity.



3. Cross-Team Collaboration

Work closely with the graphic design team to request, coordinate, and deliver images, infographics, and A+ creatives.

Partner with the marketing team to support product launches, promotions, and review-generation initiatives.

Ensure all projects are aligned with deadlines, strategy, and brand guidelines.



4. Conversion-Focused Execution

Continuously update and refine listings to improve conversion rates and customer engagement.

Maintain Amazon Brand Stores, A+ content, and ad creatives for consistency and performance.

Assist in preparing assets for influencer campaigns and external marketing pushes.



5. Project & Compliance Support

Own the administrative “heavy lifting” of flat files, bulk uploads, and catalog troubleshooting.

Flag compliance issues, unauthorized sellers, or listing errors for quick resolution.

Support market expansion efforts by preparing research and draft listings for new product opportunities.



Skills & Qualifications



2+ years experience in Amazon FBA operations, brand management, or digital marketing.

Proficient in Amazon Seller Central, flat files, listing creation, and troubleshooting.

Strong skills in SEO, keyword research, and listing optimization.

Data-driven mindset; confident in Google Sheets and reporting.

Strong communication skills to work cross-functionally with design, marketing, and operations teams.

Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple deadlines.



What Success Looks Like

Every listing across assigned brands is fully optimized, compliant, and performing.

Senior Brand Manager receives clear, accurate reports and recommendations.

Creative assets are delivered on time because you coordinated smoothly with design.



Product launches happen seamlessly with marketing and review strategies in place.

Conversions and rankings consistently improve due to your execution. If you feel you are the right fit, please respond to the job with the title " The Best Supporting Branding Manager Out There!"



Please respond directly to :

----------



Kind Regards,

Tam Ma

Co Founder

Prodigy Media

title: Virtual Sales & Admin Representative
description:
**Virtual Sales & Customer Service Assistant (SALES and Home Services Experience Required)**  



**Important – Video Interview Required (No Video = No Review)**  

To apply for this role, you **must** submit a short video (5–7 minutes) answering the questions below:



**About you & your experience**  

1. Tell me about your last one or two jobs. What did you actually do day to day, especially with phones, customers, or admin work? Why did you leave your previous jobs?  

2. What’s your best sales skill?  

3. Why does this role matter to you?



**Why you**  

4. Why should I choose you for this role instead of any other applicant? What are you the best at?



Please:  

- Introduce yourself (name, location, your time zone).  

- Answer each question clearly.  

- Upload the video (Loom, Google Drive, YouTube unlisted, etc.) and include the link with your application.  



**Applications without this video or without the required home-services experience will not be considered.**



---



### About the Role



We’re a residential window cleaning company in Oahu looking for a reliable, organized, phone-savvy virtual assistant to handle sales, customer service, and admin. You must be able to work **9:00 am – 5:00 pm, Monday–Friday (Hawaii–Aleutian Standard Time).**



Your mission is simple: **keep the schedule full, customers happy, and the back office organized.**



---



### Key Responsibilities



**1. Sales (Primary)**  

- Respond quickly to new lead inquiries by phone, text, and
.  

- Provide basic job estimates using our pricing tools and guidelines.  

- Work the sales pipeline daily: move leads through each stage and prevent dropoffs.  

- Run 7-day follow-up cadences with open estimates and unbooked leads.  

- Pull jobs forward to fill gaps in the schedule by contacting flexible customers.  

- Update CRM notes daily so every lead and client has clear, current information.



**2. Customer Service (CSR)**  

- Monitor and protect customer satisfaction on every job.  

- Handle complaints professionally; document issues and schedule go-backs (touch ups).  

- Support technicians with on-job issues (access, notes, rescheduling).  

- Send and manage booking confirmations and reminders.  

- Answer service questions about pricing, services, and availability.  

- Process reschedules/cancellations, and backfill those openings from the pipeline.  

- Monitor review responses and follow up with happy customers to get Google reviews.  

- Identify and plan simple upsell opportunities (add-on services, maintenance plans).



**3. Admin**  

- Track bonuses and commissions based on completed jobs and upsells.  

- Send internal updates and reminders to the field team (special instructions, notes).  

- Plan and document yard sign routes for technicians.  

- Process refunds once approved and record them properly.  

- Set up and track client payment plans when needed.  

- Monitor overdue invoices and run polite follow-up calls/texts; log all attempts.



---



### Requirements (Must-Haves)



- **Sales / CSR experience with a home service business in the United States**  

  - At least **1+ year** working for a U.S.-based home service company (e.g., window cleaning, pressure washing, landscaping, HVAC, plumbing, cleaning, roofing, etc.).  

  - Experience handling **inbound calls, quoting, scheduling, admin work, and customer service**.  

- Strong spoken and written English; you sound natural and confident on the phone.  

- Comfortable making and receiving a high volume of calls and messages.  

- Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to follow scripts and checklists.  

- Experience with CRM tools (HouseCall Pro a plus) and basic office tools 
, calendars, spreadsheets).  

- **Stable, reliable internet connection** and a quiet workspace.  

- Able to work **9:00 am – 5:00 pm, Monday–Friday, Hawaii–Aleutian Standard Time** consistently.



---



### Compensation



- Hourly base pay starting at **$7/hour** with growth opportunities.  

- Performance bonuses tied to:  

  - Revenue sold and collected.  

  - Service plans sold.



You’ll get training on our systems, scripts, and processes. Your work will directly impact how quickly we grow and how well we serve our customers.

title: Content & Video Editing VA
description:
ROLE OVERVIEW



We are hiring a Content & Video Editing VA to support short-form video content (TikTok, Instagram Reels) and future long-form content.



This is an execution-based role. You will not be responsible for content strategy or idea generation.



You will work from:



Pre-recorded talking-head videos



Provided scripts



Clear editing and posting instructions



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

Video Editing (Light Editing Only)



Clean jump cuts



Smooth transitions (no choppy edits)



Remove long pauses, mistakes, filler words



Add minimal on-screen text when specified



Content Preparation & Posting



Upload and schedule/post content on TikTok and Instagram



Use Metricool (or similar tools) to schedule and publish content



Follow provided captions and posting instructions exactly



Ensure formatting matches platform standards



File Management



Upload edited videos to correct Google Drive folders



Name files according to provided naming conventions



Mark tasks complete in tracking documents



REQUIRED SKILLS



Experience editing short-form vertical video (TikTok, Instagram Reels)



Proficiency in CapCut and/or Canva



Experience using Metricool (or similar scheduling tools)



Strong familiarity with Google Workspace (Docs, Drive, Sheets)



Strong attention to pacing, flow, and clean editing



Ability to follow instructions exactly (very important)



Preferred (but not required):



Familiarity with using AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT or similar) to assist with captions, formatting, or basic content support



QUALITY STANDARD (VERY IMPORTANT)



Edits must:



Feel smooth and natural



Not distract from the speaker



Match provided examples exactly



Revisions due to missed instructions or poor execution are not billable.



WORK EXPECTATIONS



Estimated 15 hours weekly depending on workload



Clear deadlines per batch



Tasks should be completed efficiently (no dragging work to fill hours)



BILLABLE TIME POLICY



Billable:



Assigned video edits



Content prep and posting



File uploads and organization



Non-billable:



Rework due to missed instructions



Waiting time



Access issues not communicated promptly



TO APPLY



Please answer the following:



What editing tools do you use?



Share 2–3 examples of short-form video edits



How do you ensure smooth pacing in talking-head videos?



Have you used Metricool or another scheduling tool before? If yes, which one?



What is your weekly availability in EST?



Please include the word “SMOOTH EDIT” at the top of your application so I know you read the full job post.

title: Proposal Designer
description:
Full-Time | Remote | Philippines | Long-Term | Competitive Pay | Great Company



We are a U.S.-based strategic communications and marketing agency working with government, public health, education, and nonprofit clients. We are seeking a Proposal Designer to support our Business Development team. This role focuses on transforming complex content into clear, structured, and visually compelling proposals. We respond to dozens of government solicitations and RFPs each quarter. Each proposal requires a tailored approach to win the business. Winning depends on our ability to convey complex topics through infographics, structured designs, interesting use of imagery, and proposal layouts that combine creativity with strategic design. 



This is not a traditional graphic design role. You will not be designing ads, logos, or social media content. You will be designing documents that win business.



What You’ll Do

You will work closely with our Business Development team to design high-quality proposal materials under tight deadlines and strict requirements.



Core Responsibilities

• Design and format RFP / proposal documents using Adobe InDesign and other tools 

• Convert dense written content into clear visual layouts, diagrams, and infographics 

• Create cover pages, section dividers, and structured document templates 

• Design process diagrams, timelines, and frameworks that explain complex ideas 

• Ensure all documents comply with strict page limits and formatting requirements 

• Format resumes, case studies, and supporting materials for proposals 

• Create clean, professional layouts in Google Slides and Google Docs when needed 

• Support rapid turnaround on proposal deadlines 

• Layout Adlobs (ad like objectives) for spec creative included in proposals 

• Maintain consistency in branding, typography, and layout across all materials 

• Provide overall art direction and creativity for business proposals 

 

Required Skills

• Strong experience with Adobe InDesign  

• Experience with Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop 

• Ability to design structured, multi-page documents (not just single graphics) 

• Strong layout, typography, and visual hierarchy skills 

• Experience turning text-heavy content into clear visual formats 

• Strong attention to detail and formatting precision 

• Ability to work under tight deadlines 

• Strong written English (for understanding and organizing content) 

 

Preferred (Nice to Have)

• Experience working with U.S. clients or agencies 

• Familiarity with Canva, Google Slides, and Google Docs 

• Experience designing infographics or process diagrams 

• Understanding of government or institutional proposals 

• Basic copy editing or content structuring ability

title: Compliance Manager
description:
Job Title:



ACL Compliance Manager (Australian Credit Licence)



Location:



Australia (e.g. Sydney / Hybrid)



Role Overview:



The ACL Compliance Manager is responsible for ensuring the organisation operates in full compliance with the National Consumer Credit Protection Act 2009 (NCCP), ASIC regulatory requirements, and internal compliance frameworks. This role oversees compliance systems, policies, monitoring, and reporting to maintain the integrity of the company’s Australian Credit Licence.



?



Key Responsibilities:



Regulatory Compliance & Governance



* Ensure ongoing compliance with ACL obligations under NCCP

* Maintain and update compliance frameworks, policies, and procedures

* Interpret and implement ASIC regulatory updates and guidance (e.g. RG 205, RG 209)

* Act as the key contact for ASIC queries, audits, and reporting obligations



Monitoring & Auditing



* Develop and manage a compliance monitoring program

* Conduct regular file reviews and audits of brokers/loan processors

* Identify breaches and implement corrective actions

* Maintain breach registers and report significant breaches to ASIC where required



Responsible Lending Oversight



* Ensure adherence to responsible lending obligations

* Review credit assessment processes and documentation standards

* Monitor loan suitability and verify income/expense assessment practices



Training & Support



* Deliver compliance training to brokers, credit reps, and staff

* Provide ongoing guidance on regulatory requirements and best practices

* Support onboarding of new credit representatives



Risk Management



* Identify compliance risks and implement mitigation strategies

* Maintain and review the compliance risk framework

* Assist with internal and external audits



Documentation & Reporting



* Maintain compliance manuals and policies

* Prepare compliance reports for senior management

* Track KPIs related to compliance breaches, audit outcomes, and remediation



?



Key Requirements:



Experience:



* Minimum 3–5 years in credit compliance, mortgage broking, or financial services

* Experience working with an ACL holder or aggregator

* Strong understanding of NCCP Act and ASIC regulations



Qualifications:



* Certificate IV in Finance & Mortgage Broking (minimum)

* Diploma of Finance & Mortgage Broking (preferred)

* Additional compliance or legal qualifications highly regarded



Knowledge:



* NCCP Act & Responsible Lending Obligations

* ASIC Regulatory Guides (RG 205, RG 209, RG 78, etc.)

* AML/CTF obligations (desirable)



?



Key Skills:



* Strong attention to detail and analytical skills

* Ability to interpret legislation and apply it practically

* Excellent communication and stakeholder management

* Audit, reporting, and documentation skills

* Problem-solving and risk assessment mindset



?



Key KPIs:



* Compliance audit pass rates

* Number and severity of breaches

* Timeliness of breach reporting

* Training completion rates

* ASIC audit outcomes



?



Reporting Line:



* Reports to: Head of Compliance / Responsible Manager / Director

* Works closely with: Brokers, Credit Reps, Operations Team



?



Nice to Have (Industry-Specific):



* Experience with non-bank lenders / alt-doc / self-employed lending

* Familiarity with aggregator platforms (e.g. ApplyOnline, Mercury, Flex)

* Experience managing credit representative networks



Skills Requirements



Technical & Regulatory Skills



* Strong knowledge of the National Consumer Credit Protection Act (NCCP) and ACL obligations

* Solid understanding of ASIC Regulatory Guides (e.g. RG 205 – Licensing, RG 209 – Responsible Lending)

* Experience with compliance frameworks, audit processes, and breach reporting

* Familiarity with responsible lending assessments, including income verification and expense analysis

* Working knowledge of AML/CTF compliance requirements (desirable)



?



Compliance & Risk Skills



* Ability to design and implement compliance monitoring programs

* Skilled in identifying, assessing, and mitigating compliance risks

* Experience maintaining breach registers and handling i
 reporting

* Strong auditing and file review capability with attention to regulatory detail



?



Analytical & Problem-Solving Skills



* High level of attention to detail and accuracy

* Ability to interpret complex legislation and translate it into practical business processes

* Strong investigative skills to identify root causes of compliance issues

* Proactive approach to risk management and continuous improvement



?



Communication & Stakeholder Management



* Excellent written and verbal communication skills

* Ability to explain complex compliance requirements in a clear, practical manner

* Strong stakeholder engagement skills across brokers, credit reps, and management

* Confidence in delivering training and compliance guidance



?



Organisational & Reporting Skills



* Strong time management and ability to handle multiple priorities

* Experience preparing compliance reports for senior management

* Ability to maintain structured documentation, policies, and procedures

* High level of accountability and professionalism



?



Technology & Systems Skills



* Experience with loan processing and CRM systems (e.g. ApplyOnline, Mercury, Flex)

* Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)

* Ability to use data to support compliance monitoring and reporting



?



Personal Attributes



* Ethical, integrity-driven, and compliance-focused mindset

* Detail-oriented with a strong sense of responsibility

* Ability to work independently and make sound judgments

* Continuous learning attitude to stay updated with regulatory changes

title: Klaviyo Email Marketing & Shopify VA
description:
