Recruitment, staffing, or permanent placement sales, if applicable



We are looking for someone who can help us grow, open new markets, and bring in consistent signed clients.

title: Client Success Manager / Account Manager — Credit Repair & Financial Coaching (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
About Wealthy Wisdom Solutions

We are a premium credit repair and financial coaching company based in the United States helping business owners get funding-ready by repairing their credit and building strong financial profiles. We currently serve 200+ active clients and are growing fast. We are looking for one highly skilled, serious, long-term tea
mber to own our client success operation completely.

This is not a casual job. This is a career opportunity for someone who wants to grow with a company that is scaling and wants to be part of something meaningful.



Position Overview

You will serve as the primary point of contact for all active clients from onboarding through the full duration of their program. You are not doing disputes — the dispute team handles that. Your job is to make sure every client is organized, informed, prepared, and receiving 5-star service at every touchpoint. You will work directly alongside the dispute team and report directly to the founder.



This Role Is ONLY for You If:

— You are a woman with a professional, warm, and commanding communication style

— Your English is excellent — spoken and written, no exceptions

— You are naturally organized and detail-obsessed — not just "good" at organizing, but you cannot function in chaos

— You take initiative and identify problems before being told

— You are reliable, consistent, and professional every single day — not just your first two weeks

— You genuinely care about client results and take pride in exceptional service

— You want to grow with a company long-term, not jump from job to job



Your Daily Responsibilities

Client Onboarding

— Welcome new clients and walk them through the onboarding process step by step

— Collect, review, and verify all required client documents (ID, proof of address, SSN card, etc.)

— Ensure all documents are legit, active, properly cropped, resized, and ready to use before passing to the dispute team — nothing incomplete passes through

— Set up and confirm client credit monitoring on SmartCredit and/or MyFreeScoreNow

— Ensure client profile in GHL is complete and accurate before dispute work begins

Client Communication & Account Management

— Manage all client communication in GHL messaging and Slack — same-day responses are required, no exceptions

— Maintain a clear and updated contact schedule for all 200+ active clients throughout the month

— Track all dispute dates, reimport dates, follow-up dates, and escalation timelines in 

— Send clients their dispute update results in a clear, professional, timely manner

— Conduct regular client check-ins to keep them informed, engaged, and on track

— Educate clients on how to read their credit report and understand what is being worked on and why

Dispute Team Support

— Fully prepare client files before handing off to the dispute team — nothing goes to the team incomplete

— Coordinate with the dispute team on timelines, updates, and escalations

— Ensure reimport dates are tracked and executed on time every cycle

Credit & Funding Knowledge

— Understand how to read a full tri-merge credit report and explain it to clients in plain language

— Guide clients on how to build their credit profile correctly alongside the dispute process

— Have working knowledge of funding readiness — what it takes for a client to qualify for business funding — and be able to communicate this clearly to clients

Platform Management — Must Be Proficient In All of the Following:

— GoHighLevel (GHL) — CRM, messaging, pipeline, client notes

— Slack — internal team communication

— 
 — task and date tracking for all client accounts

— DisputeFox — dispute workflow management and client file tracking

— SmartCredit — credit report pulls, account tagging, monitoring

— MyFreeScoreNow — client credit monitoring setup and verification

— 
 — basic familiarity required; client community management support

All platforms must stay in sync. If something is updated in one place, it is updated everywhere. There is no room for information gaps.



Standards & Expectations

— Full-time availability: Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Pacific Standard Time (PST). This is non-negotiable.

— Client messages are responded to the same day — urgency is a standard here, not an exception

— Every client file must be accurate, complete, and up to date at all times

— You will be held to clear weekly performance metrics

— You are expected to proactively identify gaps in our process and bring solutions — not just follow orders

— You will operate with professionalism that reflects a 5-star brand at all times



What You Must Know Before Applying

We currently have 200 active clients and we are behind due to previous team failures. You will be coming into a real workload that requires urgency, systems thinking, and the ability to manage high volume without dropping the ball. If high volume stresses you out or you need a slow ramp-up with no pressure — this is not the right role for you.

We are looking for the person who will be here 2–3 years from now, who helped us build something exceptional. If that is you, we want to hear from you.



To Apply, Answer All 5 of the Following — Do Not Skip Any:



Which of the required platforms have you used before and at what level — beginner, intermediate, or advanced?

Describe how you would manage 200 active clients across multiple platforms without missing dates or follow-ups.

Have you worked in credit repair or financial services before? If yes, describe your role in detail.

What does funding readiness mean to you and how would you explain it to a client?

What is your available start date and confirm you can work Monday–Friday 9AM–5PM PST full-time?



Applications that do not answer all 5 questions will not be reviewed.



Compensation: Competitive — based on experience. Discussed during interview.

Hours: Full-time | 40 hours/week | PST

Location: Remote — Philippines preferred



DM ANSWERS ON WHATS APP

title: Marketing & Studio Coordinator — Creative Studio
description:
Marketing & Studio Coordinator — Creative Studio (AU-based, fully remote)

Job Title: Marketing & Studio Coordinator Hours: Full-time, 40 hrs/week (open to part-time, 25–30 hrs/week, for the right person) Schedule: Australian business hours overlap required (approx. 7am–4pm Manila / 9am–6pm AEST). At least 6 hours of daily overlap with AEST is essential. Type: Long-term, direct hire. We want someone who stays for years. Salary: ?70,000 - ?100,000 per month, depending on experience and proof of work. Top of range available for proven brand/marketing operators with serious social and systems chops. Paid via Wise or Payoneer, every 2 weeks. Plus 13th month, paid leave, performance bonuses.





About Us

We are Nice Wolf, a small but ambitious creative studio based in Australia. We have three businesses under one roof:



Nice Wolf Studio — brand, campaign, content, and design work for clients who care about the work.

Human Terrain — film and photo production for cinematic, considered creative.

Journey Maker (Digital Locals) — an AI-powered concierge product for hotels and their guests.



Our team has decades of experience working on global brands. We move fast, we care about craft, and we already have a strong ecosystem of brands, products, clients, and tools — including in-house AI systems we've built ourselves. We are not looking for someone to build that for us. We are looking for someone to drive it forward: keeping our brands visible, our content shipping, our team motivated, and our processes sharper every week.



This is a real coordinator role with real ownership. You will be the person who keeps everything moving — and the person who looks at our already-strong ecosystem and asks "how do we make this even better?"





The Role

This role is half marketing and brand, half studio operations. You will spend roughly half your week stewarding our content, social presence, and brand consistency — and the other half keeping our projects, clients, and team running smoothly across all three businesses.



This is not a strategy role. We will give you the strategy, the brand, and the tools. Your job is to make it ship — consistently, on brand, on time — and to motivate the team around you while doing it.



You will not be expected to build AI systems. We have that covered. But you will be expected to use the tools we give you, learn fast, and look for ways to improve every workflow you touch.





What You Will Do

Marketing, Brand & Social (?50% of your time)

Own the content calendar across all our channels — LinkedIn, Instagram,
, and our blog (StoryChief). Plan it, populate it, ship it.

Live and breathe our social presence. Watch what's working. Spot what's missing. Bring ideas for what we should be posting next, not just executing what we tell you to.

Adapt design assets in Canva — resizing, copy swaps, format variants, story versions, deck cuts.

Write captions, headlines, and short-form copy that feel on brand. We will give you the voice guide. You will get sharper at it as you go.

Schedule posts and monitor engagement — replies, comments, DMs — flagging anything that needs a founder's attention.

Help coordinate small content shoots — organising assets, briefing photographers, tracking deliverables.

Keep our brand kits, templates, and asset libraries tidy in Notion and Canva so the team can move fast without going off-brand.

Notice when something looks or sounds wrong before it goes out. Have the confidence and taste to flag it.

Studio Operations & Team Coordination (?50% of your time)

Run client projects end-to-end across all three brands. Take briefs from intake to delivery. Own timelines, dependencies, and handovers. Keep the Jobs Board honest.

Keep our Notion CRM (Leads, Companies, Contacts, Deals, Jobs Board) clean and up to date.

Be the human in the loop with clients — friendly chases, polished status updates, confirmations, and the occasional Zoom call. Clients should feel looked after, not managed.

Run kickoffs, status calls, and stand-ups. Make sure the team always knows what's next and feels good about it. People should leave a meeting with you feeling clearer and more motivated, not more tired.

Help onboard new hotels onto Journey Maker through Notion workflows.

Spot processes that are leaking time and improve them — templates, SOPs, checklists, better handovers. We have the AI systems; we need someone with the operational eye to point them at the right problems.

Champion the small things that compound: a tidier file structure, a clearer naming convention, a better template, a sharper meeting agenda.





Required Skills (Must Have)

You must have all of these. Please do not apply if you do not.



Excellent written and spoken English. Your writing must be polished, warm, and on brand. Your spoken English must be clear and confident enough for client video / phone calls and internal team calls.

Brand and copywriting taste. You can write a caption, a headline, or a short-form post that sounds like a real human and stays on brand. You can adapt your voice across LinkedIn, Instagram,
, and a client
 — and you know why each is different.

Real social media coordination experience. You have managed a content calendar for a brand or agency before. You know the rhythm of social — what works, what flops, what's worth the effort. You actually enjoy it. You follow brands you admire and you can articulate why they're good.

Creative eye and brand instincts. You know good work when you see it. You can tell when a design feels off, when a caption falls flat, when something is "almost there but not quite." You don't need to be a designer — you need to have taste.

Canva proficiency. You can confidently resize, adapt, and produce design variants. You can build templates, work with brand kits, and tidy a deck.

Notion proficiency. You have used Notion as a daily working tool. You understand databases, relations, views, and templates. You can build clean workspaces and keep them organised.

Highly process-driven. You see processes everywhere. You like making things repeatable. Templates, SOPs, content calendars, and workflows are your love language. You leave every system you touch a little better than you found it.

Project coordination instincts. You can hold multiple projects in your head, track dependencies, and chase loose ends without being asked. You know when to escalate and when to handle it.

Client-facing experience. You have worked with external clients before —
s, meetings, expectations. You can be tactful when something goes wrong.

Team energy. You are the kind of person who makes the people around them feel calmer, more organised, and more motivated. You can hold a room. You can run a kickoff. You bring positive 
ntum to a project.

Proactive and improvement-minded. You don't wait to be told. You spot what's slipping, what could be sharper, what we should be doing that we're not — and you either handle it or surface it.

Detail obsession. You notice the typo, the wrong asset size, the broken link, the client who has gone quiet.

Our Tech Stack

These are the tools we use every day. Strong existing experience with any of them is a significant advantage and will move you up the list. We will teach you the ones you don't know — but the more you already know, the faster you'll be useful.



Notion — our entire operating system. CRM, Jobs Board, briefs, meeting notes, knowledge bases.

Canva — fast adaptations, decks, social cuts, templates, brand kits.

StoryChief — our blog and content distribution platform.

Slack — internal team communication.

Google Workspace — Gmail, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Calendar.

Apollo — outbound and CRM enrichment.

LinkedIn, Instagram,
 (Business) — our primary social channels.

Claude and ChatGPT — we use these heavily across the business. You don't need to be an AI builder, but day-to-day comfort with these tools is a real plus.

Nice to Have (Bonus Points)

These are not required but will move you to the top of the pile:



Strong, demonstrable experience with our tech stack above (especially Notion + Canva + a major social platform).

Experience working in or with a creative agency, design studio, or marketing team.

Experience with other content workflow tools (Buffer, Later, Hootsuite, Sprout, etc.).

Experience writing long-form content (blog posts, newsletters, case studies).

Experience with
 marketing tools (Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign, etc.).

Experience coordinating film, photo, or content shoots.

Hospitality, hotel, or travel industry experience.

Light video editing skills (CapCut, Premiere Rush, etc.) for short-form social cuts.





Who You Are

You live and breathe social. You follow brands you admire. You have opinions about content. You feel the pulse of what works.

You have a good eye for creative and brand. You don't make the work, but you know when it's good, and you know when it's not quite there yet.

You are highly process-driven. Templates, SOPs, and workflows light you up. You think about systems even when no one asked.

You are proactive. You don't wait for a brief. You bring ideas, spot what's slipping, and either handle it or surface it.

You love motivating the team. People feel calmer, more organised, and more energised after working with you. You are a quiet leader without needing the title.

You are always looking for ways to improve. You inherit a strong ecosystem and you make it stronger. You don't break what's working — you sharpen it.

You are warm and professional with clients. They feel looked after, not managed.

Who You Are NOT

To save us both time, please do not apply if:



You are looking for a side gig while you finish another full-time job.

You have only used Notion and Canva once or twice.

You have never managed a real brand's social presence (a personal account doesn't count).

You think social media coordination is just scheduling posts. There's craft in this work.

You are uncomfortable getting on a video call with English-speaking clients.

You expect to be told exactly what to do every day.

You consider yourself a strategist or creative director. This is a coordinator role with ownership, not a strategy role.

You'd rather work alone than energise a team.





How to Apply

We will only consider applications that follow these instructions exactly. This is your first test of attention to detail.



Send your application with the following, in this order:



1. Subject line: Marketing & Studio Coordinator — [Your Full Name]



2. A short note (under 200 words): Why does this role interest you? What about Nice Wolf caught your eye? Be specific. We will not read generic cover letters.



3. Your CV or LinkedIn URL.



4. Two work samples (this is important — we want to see both halves of the role):



a) A brand / social / content sample. Show us one of:



A social media account you have managed for a brand (with link)

A content calendar you built and ran

A caption, headline, or short-form copy piece you wrote

A Canva design or template you created

A small campaign you helped ship — what was the goal, what did you do, what was the result



b) A systems / operations sample. Show us one of:



A Notion workspace you built or improved (public link or screenshots)

A process or SOP you built that saved time or improved quality

A template, checklist, or workflow you created and rolled out to a team

A small win where you spotted something broken and fixed it



For both, tell us what was broken or missing, what you did about it, and what changed because of it. Even small examples are fine. We care more about how you think than how big the project was.



5. A 60–90 second Loom video introducing yourself. Tell us:



Your name and where you are based

Your current role and what you do day to day

One thing you've shipped that you're proud of (brand, content, or systems)

Why you would be good at this hybrid role



Do not script it. Just be yourself. We use this to check English, presence, and personality.



6. The word "TERRAIN" somewhere in your application. This is how we know you read this job ad properly.



7. Your expected monthly salary in PHP and your earliest start date.





What Happens Next

We review every application that follows the instructions above. Applications that skip steps will not be reviewed.

Shortlisted candidates will get a paid trial task (4–6 hours, paid at your full rate). The task will involve both a content piece and an operational piece, so we can see both halves of the role.

Top candidates from the trial will be invited to a 30-minute Zoom interview with the founder.

Final decision within 2–3 weeks of application.



We are excited to find the right person. Take your time with your application — we will take our time reading it.



Apply via OnlineJobs.ph. We do not respond to messages on other platforms.

title: Executive Assistant
description:
Overview

Right now, we're looking for a dynamic and talented Executive Assistant to join us on our mission.

This is a high-impact role, reporting directly to the CEO, where you will play a key role in managing the day-to-day business operation, internal work-management system, and our CEO's calendar to enable us to continue supporting the growth of our clients.



How you'll contribute:

Support our CEO: Work as an extension of our CEO to assist in and take responsibility for a range of functions and tasks, including calendar management,
 inbox triage, task prioritisation, research projects, travel arrangements, and more.



Systems & processes: Develop, document, and optimise robust processes to manage repeat tasks and communication with our internal team and 3rd party suppliers/providers, as well as setting up workflows using Asana for transparency and maintaining and organising our virtual office tools like Google Drive, Canva, Slack, etc.



Review invoices: Carefully review invoices from our suppliers and service providers to ensure the invoices are accurate.

System admin: Help drive and manage the practice management tool (follow up tea
mbers, identify bottlenecks, coordinate, etc).



Tech stack admin: Act as administrator of our tech stack, with full access rights, ensuring that the tech stack is always working and that everyone in the team has the right access permissions they need to the technologies that power our business.

General administration: Conduct general administrative tasks, client invoicing, basic bookkeeping, keeping the business and teams organized, following up promises made by people, and ensuring a high flow of communication between teams and service providers.



Ad hoc tasks: Manage and complete ad hoc tasks and projects as needed, provide general support to the team for overflow of work (bookkeeping, data-entry, etc.), and manage a range of tasks and projects, often simultaneously.



What you'll need to succeed in this role:

-Stickler for details and you're highly organized - good at organizing others too!

-Proven track record as an Executive Assistant with more than 3 years experience.

-You have general/basic bookkeeping skills.

-Comprehensive understanding of professional services business operations.

-Considerate person and know how to navigate human relationships diplomatically to achieve objectives.

-You work fast and pride yourself in getting a lot done in a short amount of time.

-Highly collaborative and communicative. You love working with others and are good at taking and giving instructions in order to achieve a common objective.

-You have great English speaking and writing capability.

-You're growth-minded and take responsibility for your own professional development, including staying up to date with the latest industry trends.



Bonus:

You are experienced in using practice management software.

You have general/basic bookkeeping skills.

title: Marketing Operations Assistant (HubSpot / CRM / Automation)
description:
About the Role

We are a U.S.-based B2B software company in the energy industry.



You will work directly with our Head of Marketing to execute marketing operations, campaigns, and CRM management inside HubSpot.



This is not a social media or content-only role.

Your job is to translate marketing direction into clean, structured execution inside our systems.





What You’ll Be Responsible For

1. HubSpot CRM Management

- Clean and maintain contact and company data (duplicates, invalid
s, lifecycle stages) 

- Manage lists, properties, and segmentation 

- Perform ongoing data hygiene and enrichment (
, NeverBounce) 

- Ensure CRM accuracy and usability 



2. Marketing Automation

- Build and maintain workflows (lead routing, nurture sequences, follow-ups) 

- Execute drip campaigns (including state-specific campaigns) 

- Support outbound automation 

- Troubleshoot workflow and data issues 



3. Campaign Execution

- Build and send
 campaigns, templates, and snippets 

- Support marketing campaigns and promotions 

- Assist with event follow-up campaigns 

- Connect tools (Appcues, forms, etc.) into HubSpot 



4. Lead Management

- Maintain and improve lead scoring (with guidance) 

- Review inbound leads and ensure proper routing 

- Identify and clean low-quality or invalid leads 



5. Reporting & Data

- Prepare reports on campaign performance 

- Ensure tracking and data consistency 

- Identify issues (broken workflows, missing data, etc.) 



6. Systems Support

- Assist with integrations (QuickBooks sync, internal tools) 

- Help maintain clean data flow between systems 





What Success Looks Like

- HubSpot data is clean, organized, and reliable 

- Campaigns are executed on time with minimal errors 

- Marketing operations run smoothly without constant oversight 

- You can take direction and turn it into completed work 





Requirements

- 2+ years experience with HubSpot OR similar CRM , and Marketing operations, admin, or automation support 

- Strong attention to detail 

- Strong written English 

- Ability to follow instructions and work independently 





Who This Role Is For

You like working with systems, structure, and data 

You are strong at execution and follow-through 

You can take direction and turn it into finished work 





Who This Role Is NOT For

Social media-only marketers 

Content writers without CRM experience 

Applicants who need constant supervision 





How to Apply (Read Carefully)

We receive a high volume of applications. We only review candidates who follow instructions exactly.



Step 1: Subject Line

Use ONE of the following Enerex core values as your subject line:



Earn Trust 

We > Me 

Own It 

Create Simplicity 

Celebrate Progress 



If your subject line does not match one of these exactly, your application will not be reviewed.



Step 2: Explain Your Choice

At the top of your application, answer:

Which value did you choose, and why does it resonate most with you? Use a real example.



Step 3: Answer These Questions

- What is your internet speed? Include a screenshot from speedtest.net. 

- Do you currently have another job? If yes: What do you do?  How many hours per week? 

- You log into HubSpot and find duplicate contacts , missing
s , and unclear lifecycle stages. What exact steps would you take to clean this up? (Be specific.)

- Give an example of how you improved or simplified a process. 

- What actions do you take to build trust with a remote manager? 



Include the word “structured” somewhere in your application.



Step 4: Resume & Short Video (2–3 minutes)

- Attached your updated resume.

- Record a Loom video answering:

>>> Why this role is a good fit for you 

>>> Your experience with HubSpot or similar tools 





Final Notes

- This is a long-term role with growth opportunity 

- Performance-based increases after 90 days 

- We value reliability, attention to detail, and accountability

title: L1 Data Operations Specialist (Enterprise AI Tech)
description:
JOB DESCRIPTION:



About TaskMaster: The End of the Standard BPO

We do not run a traditional BPO. We are pioneers of HIaaS (Human Infrastructure as a Service). Global enterprises are spending billions on Artificial Intelligence, but their AI models are hallucinating and failing because the data feeding them is a chaotic, unstructured mess.



TaskMaster solves the "AI Impact Gap." We deploy elite, fully managed 2-2-1 Pods natively into enterprise infrastructures to clean, sanitize, and validate data before it hits the AI model.



We operate on the Triangle of Wealth (Faith, Love, and Hope). Our mission is not just to fix AI; it is to elevate Filipino professionals from standard outsourcing grinds into high-paying, dignified, "NASA-Standard" technical careers.



The Mission: The Frontline Sentry

You are the frontline of our Zero-Defect Execution. Your job is to take chaotic, unstructured enterprise data (messy PDFs, medical records, commercial leases) and manually ingest, normalize, and format it into pristine, structured datasets. You prevent AI hallucinations before they happen.



Key Roles & Responsibilities:



High-Volume Data Normalization: Execute strict SOPs to format raw data into structured schemas (CSV, JSON, specific CRM fields).



Protocol Execution: Follow highly technical "runbooks" without deviation to ensure absolute data uniformity.



Environment Security: Operate exclusively within secure Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) or VPNs, strictly adhering to our Zero-Data-Retention (ZDR) policy.



Quality Assurance: Perform initial self-QA on all data batches before passing them to the L2 layer.



Expected Results & Outcomes:



Maintain a 99%+ Data Formatting Accuracy Rate.



Consistently hit daily throughput and Service Level Agreement (SLA) turnaround times.



Zero compliance or data security breaches.



Required Skills & Mindset:



Insane Attention to Detail: You spot the missing comma, the misaligned column, and the formatting error that others ignore. You thrive on structure, repetition, and flawless execution.



Technical Speed: High-speed data processing, typing proficiency, and mastery of basic spreadsheet logic (Excel/Google Sheets).



Compliance Experience (Highly Preferred): Prior experience operating in highly regulated, secure environments (SOC 2, HIPAA, or GDPR). You understand the gravity of handling sensitive data.



Strict Hardware & Workspace Requirements (Non-Negotiable):



Minimum Intel Core i5 / AMD Ryzen 5 processor or equivalent (Apple M1+).



16GB RAM minimum (required to run secure VDI environments smoothly).



Dual monitors.



Highly stable wired internet connection (50mbps+).



Backup power supply (UPS/Generator) and backup internet connection.



How to Apply:

We are hiring for extreme data fidelity, which means we test for attention to detail before we even read your resume.



To ensure you have read this entire brief and possess the exact focus required for this pod, you must start your application message/cover letter with the exact phrase: "TaskMaster Alpha ready to deploy." Applications that do not begin with this exact phrase will be immediately archived without review.

We do not run a traditional BPO. We are pioneers of HIaaS (Human Infrastructure as a Service). Global enterprises are spending billions on Artificial Intelligence, but their AI models are hallucinating and failing because the data feeding them is a chaotic, unstructured mess.



TaskMaster solves the "AI Impact Gap." We deploy elite, fully managed 2-2-1 Pods natively into enterprise infrastructures to clean, sanitize, and validate data before it hits the AI model.



We operate on the Triangle of Wealth (Faith, Love, and Hope). Our mission is not just to fix AI; it is to elevate Filipino professionals from standard outsourcing grinds into high-paying, dignified, "NASA-Standard" technical careers.



The Mission: The Tech Translator

You are the human-in-the-loop (HITL) anomaly detection layer. When an AI model flags a low-confidence output, or when the L1 team encounters a complex edge case they cannot process, it escalates to you. You inject human logic, resolve the anomaly, and train the model not to make the same mistake twice.



Key Roles & Responsibilities:



Edge-Case Resolution: Analyze and resolve complex data anomalies that break standard L1 protocols.



RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback): Review AI outputs, score them for accuracy/bias, and provide contextual feedback to improve the model.



Prompt Auditing & Testing: Test different input structures to identify why an AI model is hallucinating or drifting.



Data Manipulation: Use basic scripting to parse, filter, or manipulate messy datasets before they enter the pipeline.



Escalation Management: Package unresolved, critical pipeline failures and communicate them clearly to the L3 Pod Architect.



Expected Results & Outcomes:



Maintain an Edge-Case Resolution Time of under 1 hour per ticket.



Measurably reduce "model drift" and repeat AI errors through highly accurate RLHF grading.



Ensure 100% qualitative accuracy of the data passing from the Pod into the client's live AI environment.



Required Skills & Mindset:



Advanced Analytical Thinking: You don't just ask "what is broken?", you ask "why did the logic fail?"



Technical Foundation: Basic to intermediate knowledge of Python and/or SQL for data manipulation.



AI Fluency: Understanding of how Large Language Models (LLMs) work, what causes hallucinations, and the basics of model training/fine-tuning.



Compliance Advantage: Deep familiarity with data compliance frameworks (SOC 2, HIPAA, PII handling). You ensure that the AI model's output does not violate enterprise regulatory standards.



Mindset: You are a problem-solver who operates with extreme ownership in high-ambiguity situations.



The TaskMaster DNA (Required):



Extreme Ownership: You do not make excuses. You own the result.



Zero-Defect Execution: You believe that "good enough" is the enemy of greatness.



Keep Your Promise: If you commit to an SLA, a team member, or a client, you deliver.



Strict Hardware & Workspace Requirements (Non-Negotiable):



Minimum Intel Core i5 / AMD Ryzen 5 processor or equivalent.



16GB RAM minimum (required to run secure VDI environments smoothly).



Dual monitors.



Highly stable wired internet connection (50mbps+).



Backup power supply (UPS/Generator) and backup internet connection.



How to Apply:

We are hiring for extreme data fidelity, which means we test for attention to detail before we even read your resume.



To ensure you have read this entire brief and possess the exact focus required for this pod, you must start your application message/cover letter with the exact phrase: "TaskMaster QA Bravo ready to deploy." Applications that do not begin with this exact phrase will be immediately archived without review.

title: Video Editor for E-Commerce Brand (TikTok / Meta Ads)
description:
Job Title:

Video Editor for E-Commerce Brand (TikTok / Meta Ads)

Job Type:

Full-time / Part-time (open to both)

Compensation:

To be discussed based on experience



About Us

We are a fast-growing e-commerce brand selling laundry products in the US market. We run paid advertising on Meta and TikTok and are looking for a talented video editor who can create scroll-stopping ad creatives that actually convert. If you understand how performance content works and you have a sharp eye for what makes people stop, watch, and buy, we want to hear from you.



What You Will Be Doing



Editing short-form video ads (60 to 135 seconds) for TikTok and Meta using UGC-style footage

Sourcing raw stock content from TikTok, Instagram Reels and CapCut libraries

Creating and syncing voiceovers using AI tools like HeyGen or ElevenLabs

Adding clean, readable subtitles that are manually reviewed and corrected

Analyzing competitor ads to understand what makes them perform and applying those insights to your own edits

Following a provided script and brief to produce a finished ad ready for launch

Delivering video in 9:16 format at 1080x1920 resolution

Making revision rounds quickly based on feedback





What We Are Looking For



At least 1 year of experience editing short-form video content

Strong understanding of TikTok and Meta ad formats

Experience with CapCut, Adobe Premiere, or DaVinci Resolve

Familiarity with AI voiceover tools such as HeyGen or ElevenLabs

Good eye for pacing, text overlays, and visual storytelling

Ability to study a competitor video and understand why it is working

Reliable internet connection and ability to meet deadlines

Strong attention to detail, especially with subtitles and audio sync

Good communication in English





Nice to Have



Experience working with e-commerce or direct-to-consumer brands

Understanding of the pain-agitate-solve ad structure

Experience running or working alongside paid media campaigns





How We Work

We provide you with a full script, a competitor video to reference, and clear pacing guidelines. Your job is to bring it to life. We give honest feedback quickly and reward editors who take initiative and improve with every round. If your first video performs well, this becomes a long-term role with growing responsibility.



HOW TO APPLY - PLEASE READ CAREFULLY

We do not hire based on portfolios alone. To be considered for this role you must complete a short paid test project. This is how we find out if you are the real deal.

Here is exactly what to do:







Step 1 - Watch the reference video

We will send you a competitor video ad upon request. Watch it at least 3 times. Study why it works. Look at the hook, the pacing, the proof 
nt, the subtitles, and how the product is introduced.

Step 2 - Edit the test video

Using the script and brief we provide, create a complete video ad of 2 minutes in length. Requirements:



Source UGC-style footage from TikTok or similar platforms

Generate the voiceover using HeyGen or ElevenLabs with an American female voice

Add manually reviewed subtitles, bold white text with black outline, maximum 5 words per line

Deliver in 9:16 format, 1080x1920 resolution

No copyrighted music, use Epidemic Sound or CapCut's free library



Step 3 - Submit your video

Send us the finished video along with a short note explaining the creative choices you made and what you took from the competitor video analysis.

If your video meets our standard, you are hired immediately.



To get started, send us:



A short introduction (3 to 5 sentences maximum)

Confirmation that you are willing to complete the test project

Your availability and expected monthly rate



We respond to every serious applicant within 3 business days.



We are not looking for someone who can make videos look pretty. We are looking for someone who understands why people buy. If that is you, apply now.

title: Virtual Assistant - Home Services/Painting Company (Part Time, 15-20 hrs/week)
description:
About Us



Color Waves Painting is a growing residential and commercial painting company based in the Milwaukee, Wisconsin area. We do high-quality interior and exterior work for homeowners in neighborhoods like Brookfield, Elm Grove, Fox Point, and Mequon. We're scaling up and need a sharp, reliable VA to help us capture more leads and keep our follow-up tight.



What You'll Do



Answer inbound calls and texts from potential customers during business hours (CST). Schedule estimates while logging every lead immediately into Jobber (our job management software) with all contact info and job details. Follow up on open quotes at the 48-hour and 5-day marks using provided scripts. Assist with outbound outreach to property managers, condo associations, and general contractors (scripts provided). Send review request messages to completed customers. Handle basic scheduling and calendar coordination. Flag urgent leads to the owner immediately via text.



What We're Looking For



Excellent written and spoken English — you will be speaking with American homeowners on the phone. Previous experience supporting a home services company (plumbing, HVAC, landscaping, painting, roofing, etc.) is a strong plus. Comfort with CRM or job management tools (Jobber, ServiceTitan, Housecall Pro, or similar). Reliable internet connection and a quiet workspace. Available Monday–Friday, roughly 8am–5pm Central Time (Milwaukee). Self-starter who flags problems early rather than letting them pile up.



Pay & Hours

15–20 hours per week to start, with potential to grow to full-time. $4–$7/hour depending on experience. Paid weekly via Wise. Pay review at 30 days if performance is strong.



To Apply



Send a voice/video introduction (1–2 minutes) telling us about your experience with home services or trades companies. Answer this one question in your message: "What CRM or job management software have you used, and what did you use it for?"



Applications without a voice/video intro and the screening question answered will not be reviewed.

title: Full-Time Executive Admin / Team Lead (Real Estate Media Company)
description:
Full-Time Executive Admin / Team Lead — Crosby Productions



==================================================

*** HOW TO APPLY — READ THIS FIRST ***

==================================================



To be considered, you MUST submit ALL THREE of the following. Applications missing any item will NOT be reviewed — no exceptions.



STEP 1: Submit your updated resume.



STEP 2: Answer this question in writing:

"Describe a time you solved a problem at work before your manager noticed it. What did you do?"



STEP 3: Record a selfie video (1–2 minutes max).

- Tell us who you are

- Why you want to work for Crosby Productions

- Why we should hire you

- Upload your video here: 



>> Applications missing the resume, written answer, OR selfie video will not be reviewed. <<



==================================================



SALARY: $2,000/month (Full-Time)

HOURS: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Eastern Standard Time

TYPE: Remote / Work From Home



ABOUT THE ROLE

Crosby Productions is a fast-growing real estate media production company based in the United States. We deliver professional photography, videography, drone, and 3D tour services to real estate agents and brokerages across the country.



We are hiring a highly organized, detail-oriented Full-Time Executive Admin to join our core team. This is a day-to-day operational role — you will be embedded in our workflow, managing communications, scheduling, coordination, and team oversight. We are especially interested in candidates who already lead or manage a small team and can bring that leadership experience into our operation.



This is a long-term position with real growth potential for the right person.



RESPONSIBILITIES

- Manage day-to-day scheduling, bookings, and calendar coordination

- Handle inbound and outbound phone calls professionally on behalf of the company

- Communicate with clients, vendors, and tea
mbers clearly and promptly

- Track projects, follow up on outstanding items, and keep operations running smoothly

- Manage and organize team tasks and workflows

- Monitor and respond to
s and messages in a timely manner

- Identify problems before they escalate and proactively find solutions

- Maintain records, logs, and internal documentation with extreme accuracy

- Support leadership with administrative and operational tasks as needed



A DAY IN THE LIFE

Here's what a typical day looks like as our Lead Admin:



- 8:50 AM — You're logged in and ready 10 minutes early. You review the day's shoot schedule, confirm all bookings, and flag anything that needs attention before the team hits the road.

- 9:00 AM — Morning check-in. You make sure every photographer knows where they're going, what they're shooting, and what the client expects.

- 9:30–11:30 AM — You're managing incoming calls and messages. Agents are booking shoots, asking about delivery times, and requesting changes. You handle it all calmly and professionally.

- 11:30 AM–1:00 PM — You're coordinating with the editing team on turnarounds, chasing down any missing information from shoots that morning, and keeping the client pipeline moving.

- 1:00–3:00 PM — You're deep in admin work: updating our scheduling system, organizing files, following up on unpaid invoices, and handling any loose ends from the morning.

- 3:00–4:30 PM — You prep for the next day. Confirm tomorrow's shoots, send client reminders, and make sure the team has everything they need.

- 4:30–5:00 PM — Final wrap-up. You send a quick status update to leadership, flag anything unresolved, and close out the day clean.



No two days are exactly the same — and that's part of what makes this role exciting. You have to be adaptable, proactive, and always thinking one step ahead.



YOU WON'T SUCCEED IN THIS ROLE IF...

- You wait to be told what to do — we need someone who sees problems and solves them without being asked

- You struggle with spoken English — you will be on real phone calls with U.S. clients from day one

- You're not comfortable with technology — we move fast and use digital tools constantly

- You miss deadlines or lose track of details — in real estate media, timing is everything

- You shut down under pressure — busy seasons are intense and we need someone who stays cool

- You're not using AI in your daily workflow — we expect our team to leverage AI tools (ChatGPT, Notion AI, etc.) to work smarter and faster. If you're not already doing this, this isn't the right fit.

- You treat this like a side job — we need full commitment, every day, on time



AI SKILLS REQUIRED

We are a modern, fast-moving company and we expect our team to use AI tools to work efficiently. You should be comfortable using tools like ChatGPT, Notion AI, or similar to:

- Draft and proofread communications

- Summarize notes and action items

- Research and problem-solve quickly

- Automate repetitive tasks where possible



If you're not currently using AI in your workflow, please take some time to get familiar before applying.



REQUIREMENTS

- Fluent in English — spoken and written (non-negotiable; you will be on live phone calls with U.S. clients)

- Proven experience in an admin, operations, or executive assistant role

- Comfortable taking and making phone calls in real time

- Experience managing or coordinating a team



PREFERRED (BONUS POINTS)

- You already manage a small VA team and can bring that leadership into this role

- Experience in the real estate, media, or photography industry

- Familiarity with scheduling software, CRMs, or project management tools



COMPENSATION & GROWTH

- Starting pay: $2,000/month (full-time)

- Long-term position with growth potential as the company scales

- We are looking for a long-term partner, not just a contractor

title: Sales-Heavy B2B Appointment Setter | Commercial Cleaning | U.S. Hours
description:
I run a lead generation agency for commercial cleaning / janitorial companies, and I am hiring an experienced B2B cold caller / appointment setter.



This is not a general VA, admin, or customer service role. This is a sales-heavy outbound calling role.



You will be calling direct lines of decision makers such as property managers, facility managers, office managers, operations managers, business owners, medical offices, churches, schools, warehouses, and commercial buildings.



The goal is to book walkthroughs, quote calls, or cleaning service appointments for commercial cleaning companies.



Responsibilities:

- Make high-volume outbound calls using our dialer/CRM

- Call direct decision-maker numbers

- Open conversations naturally and confidently

- Handle objections like “send me info,” “we already have someone,” and “not interested”

- Qualify prospects

- Book walkthroughs or quote calls

- Leave detailed notes after every call

- Provide daily updates and call summaries

- Follow the script but also be able to think and sell naturally



Requirements:

- Must have B2B cold calling or appointment-setting experience

- Must have sales experience, not just customer service experience

- Must be comfortable making calls during 9 AM–5 PM Pacific Time

- Must be able to use our dialer/CRM

- Must allow call recording for quality review

- Must send a voice sample or previous call recording

- Must have strong English and a confident phone voice

- Must be reliable and communicate clearly every day



Experience in commercial cleaning, janitorial, facility services, property management, real estate, roofing, solar, insurance, merchant services, or other B2B sales roles is a plus.



To apply, please answer these questions:



1. Do you have B2B cold calling or appointment-setting experience?

2. Have you ever booked appointments for commercial cleaning, janitorial, facility services, or local service businesses?

3. Can you work 9 AM–5 PM Pacific Time?

4. Are you comfortable using our dialer with call recording?

5. How many outbound calls can you realistically make per day?

6. What is your hourly rate?

7. Please include a voice sample or previous call recording if available.



Please also record a quick voice sample saying this opener:



“Hi, this is [Your Name]. I know I’m calling out of the blue, but I was trying to see if you’re the person who handles cleaning or janitorial services for the building?”



Then handle this objection:



“We already have someone.”

title: Technical Report Writer & AI Transcription Specialist (Building Surveys)
description:
About Us:

We are a premier building consultancy company that surveys commercial premises across New Zealand and the Pacific. We are looking to outsource the transcription and formatting of our highly technical building survey reports to a detail-oriented freelancer or agency.



The Role:

You will be responsible for translating raw video footage from our building surveyors into polished, professional reports using Google Workspace and AI tools. We have a highly documented, step-by-step Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) via Scribe that you will follow.  



Your general workflow will involve:

- Accessing unlisted YouTube playlists of our site surveys. 

- Extracting video transcripts with precise timestamps using external tools. 

- Utilizing AI platforms (like Claude or Gemini) with specific prompting to map the transcript data into our proprietary report tables.  

- Manually verifying AI outputs by cross-referencing timestamps to capture and insert accurately formatted (4:3) screenshots of defects or observations into Google Docs.  



Requirements:

AI Problem-Solving Skills: You must be highly proficient in prompting and managing AI tools to format technical data.

High Attention to Detail: You are matching specific visual defects in a video to their corresponding text descriptions. Accuracy is non-negotiable.  

Tech Stack: Proficiency in Google Docs, Google Sheets, and basic screenshot/cropping tools.  

Excellent written English and comprehension of building/construction terminology is a massive plus.



Compensation & Scope:

This is a fixed-rate role at $180.00 USD per completed report.

The faster and more accurately a report is completed, the higher your effective hourly rate becomes.

We will begin with a paid trial period (3-4 reports). 

Upon successful completion, we are looking for a long-term, ongoing partnership.



How to Apply:

Please review the attached example report to understand the final deliverable we expect. To ensure you have read this post completely, please start your proposal with the phrase: "Blueprint 2024". Proposals without this phrase will be automatically declined.

Please briefly mention your experience with AI tools and formatting technical documents in your cover letter.

title: Real estate wholesaling caller
description: Looking for a cold caller for 2-3 months to see if you have what it takes to run my operation.

title: Rockstar Real Estate Wholesaling Virtual Assistant / Cold Caller
description:
We are looking for a reliable, hungry, and organized Virtual Assistant / Cold Caller to join our real estate wholesaling business.



This role is for someone fast and efficient who understands the USA real estate wholesaling business, knows how to speak with motivated sellers, run comparables, and is comfortable working inside Go High Level CRM and creating automations/workflows



You will be helping with seller outreach, follow-ups, CRM updates, lead management, and daily communication with our team.



This is not just a dialing job. We are looking for someone who can think, ask good questions, stay organized, and help us move leads from cold conversations to warm opportunities.



1. Strong Follow-Up System



Most deals do not close on the first call. The VA should know how to follow up without sounding pushy.



They should be able to:



Call back warm leads

Send short text follow-ups

Track next follow-up dates

Keep notes on seller objections

Revive old leads inside the CRM



A good cold caller does not just “make calls.” They create a follow-up machine.



2. Lead Qualification and Seller Motivation



The VA should understand that not every seller is a deal.



They must be able to identify:



Why the seller may want to sell

How soon they want to sell

What problem they are trying to solve

Whether the property needs repairs

Whether there are taxes, liens, vacancy, probate, tenants, divorce, foreclosure, or other issues

Whether the seller’s price is realistic



This helps protect your time and keeps you focused on the right opportunities.



3. CRM Discipline and Daily Reporting



A cold caller can be friendly, but if the CRM is messy, the business becomes messy.



The assistant should update every lead properly:



Correct pipeline stage

Clear call notes

Proper tags

Follow-up date

Seller motivation

Asking price

Property condition

Call outcome

Next action



They should also send a simple daily report showing:



Number of calls made

Number of conversations

Number of warm leads

Number of appointments booked

Top seller objections

Leads needing our review



You must have experience with:



Real estate wholesaling in the USA market

Cold calling motivated sellers

Go High Level CRM

Lead follow-up and pipeline management

Speaking clearly and professionally with homeowners

Take detailed notes inside CRM

Asking qualifying questions about property condition, motivation, timeline, and asking price



You Must not: 



Be working for another company during the hours you are hired with us

SHIFT

Must have overlap with U.S. Mountain Time

(Either early morning or late afternoon Philippine time)



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

1. Project Management & Operations

- Maintain clean and updated CRM/project tracking systems

- Monitor timelines and ensure projects are delivered on schedule

3. Team Coordination

mbers (audio engineer, studio coordinator)

- Assign tasks, set deadlines, and ensure accountability

- Follow up on pending deliverables and milestones



4. Production & Admin Support

5.  Distribution & Reporting

6. Outreach & Marketing Support

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

title: Chief of staff
description:
Location: Remote

Type: 20-30hr/wk

Overview

We’re looking for a Chief of Staff to act as a force multiplier to the CEO. This role is focused on driving execution, bringing structure to ambiguity, and ensuring key priorities across operations, customer experience, and internal accountability are actually delivered.

This is not an administrative role. You will be expected to think independently, own outcomes, and move fast with imperfect information.



What You’ll Do

1. Drive Accountability Across the Team

Track key priorities and ensure follow-through across functions (ops, sales, finance)

Identify bottlenecks and push issues to resolution

Hold owners accountable without creating friction

2. Own Problem Solving & Investigations

Take messy issues (e.g., customer complaints, operational misses) and break them down

Identify root causes, not just symptoms

Propose and execute solutions

3. Manage Customer Communication (Selective)

Draft clear, structured responses for sensitive or complex situations

Step in when escalation or synthesis is needed

4. Build and Maintain Dashboards

Create simple, actionable dashboards for business performance

Turn raw data into insights and decisions

Continuously improve visibility into key metrics

5. Act as an Execution Partner to the CEO

Turn ideas into action plans

Drive projects end-to-end with minimal oversight

Reduce the CEO’s involvement in day-to-day execution



What We’re Looking For

You are highly structured

You can take ambiguity and create clarity quickly

You naturally break problems into components and action steps

You have a strong bias for action

You move things forward without waiting for perfect information

You default to doing, not discussing

You communicate with precision

You can summarize complex situations into a few clear points

Your writing is concise, structured, and decision-oriented

You take ownership

You close loops and ensure outcomes, not just tasks

You follow through across multiple stakeholders

You are comfortable with ambiguity

You can operate in a fast-moving, imperfect environment

You don’t need constant direction



Nice to Have (but not required)

Experience in operations, consulting, or a high-growth environment

Strong Excel / Google Sheets skills

Experience building dashboards or working with data

Familiarity with tools like Notion, Airtable, Zapier



What Success Looks Like

Fewer issues require CEO involvement

Team execution becomes more consistent and predictable

Problems are identified and solved earlier

Clear visibility into business performance improves decision-making

“If you’re looking for a purely administrative or coordination role, this is not a fit.”





How to Apply

Please submit:

1. Put “CKS chief” at the beginning of the subject of your message

2. A short introduction (max 200 words)

3. An example of a complex problem you structured and solved

4. (Optional but encouraged) A sample of a dashboard, analysis, or written communication

5. (Optional) If the salary proposed does not align with your expectation, what is your expected salary range

title: Video Editor
description:
Job Description

Zander Media is hiring a video editor to join our team. Responsibilities will include editing videos in order to post-produce vertical videos, which will be played on social media.



**Responsibilities**

Edit raw footage necessary to post-produce YouTube videos, YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels, and TikTok video featuring Robin Zander for his relevant social media channels.

Edit raw footage necessary to post-produce YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels, and TikTok video featuring other Zander Media clients for social media channels.

Self-assign deadlines and release schedules, manage your time, and oversee others on the Zander Media team (including Robin) hit those deadlines.

Stay current with YouTube and other platform best practices and educate the Zander Media team about what works.

Track metrics (views, clickthrough rate, etc.) relevant to YouTube, and implement tactics for increasing engagement with Robin’s content.

Write initial headlines and copy that promote these short form videos.

Draft thumbnails for each short video.



**Workflow**

The Zander Media team will provide you with long form (30+ minutes) or shorter videos, which you will review and note specific aspects that you think will make compelling short videos.

Over time, this review process will not be necessary and you can begin editing without a review

You will cut together multiple clips into a seamless 15-60 second video. Occasionally, these videos might be much longer.

You will add in additional b-roll that adds visual effects or visual interest to the video.

You will upload the video to Frame.io for review.

You will be responsible for creating a thumbnail which shows what this video is about. 

You will draft several ideas for titles and descriptions of the video.

You may also be responsible for posting the video on the relevant social media platform or platforms.



**Samples**



These are some samples of short form video which we would like to you emulate:

Robin's long form podcasts: 

Robin's short podcast shorts: 



These videos are Tim, talking directly to camera. The video editor has added animated visual elements, music, and effects.






 

Ryan Holiday

These videos are recorded by Ryan on his phone. The video editor has cut between relevant words, making the video a tight as possible, and then added images, which pop onto the screen an accentuate Ryan’s words.








 





**About Zander Media**

Zander Media is a small but growing media production and brand strategy studio based in the Bay Area, California. We serve startups, large corporate clients, and local small businesses with our strategy and storytelling. 



We value company culture and community highly, and are seeking a video editor to join our team.

title: Social Media Marketing VA
description:
Social Media Marketing VA (Full-Time, Remote)



Platforms: Instagram, X, TikTok, Pinterest, LinkedIn

Schedule: Monday–Friday | 9:00 AM–5:00 PM EST (New York Time)



About the Role



We are hiring full-time Social Media Marketing VA to manage and grow client social media accounts across Instagram, X, TikTok, Pinterest, and LinkedIn.



This is a hands-on execution role, not a content-only or automation-based position.



All growth is done manually, using real engagement, strategic targeting, and platform-safe best practices.

No bots. No automation tools. No shortcuts.



Each analyst manages approximately 10 client accounts and is responsible for executing daily growth activities, tracking performance, and maintaining full visibility through documentation and reporting.



If you are detail-oriented, disciplined, and enjoy structured social media growth work, this role is a strong long-term fit.



Important Notes (Please Read Carefully)



No bots, automation tools, or third-party growth apps

All actions are manual and follow strict internal SOPs

Daily documentation, screenshots, and reporting are mandatory

This is a full-time role only (not freelance or part-time)



What You’ll Do

Execute manual organic growth across assigned platforms

Follows / unfollows

Likes and comments

Story interactions, profile visits, community engagement

Follow client-approved growth strategies and daily limits

Manage and update daily tracking spreadsheets

Monitor account health, activity, and potential restrictions

Submit weekly summaries and performance updates

Collaborate with Account Manager and Content teams when needed



Job Requirements



To be considered, you must:



Be available full-time only (Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM–5:00 PM EST)

Have reliable high-speed internet

Be comfortable with daily Slack communication

Have a verified identity

Own both a computer and a mobile phone (required for account access)

Be willing to follow strict SOPs and documentation rules

Commit to long-term employment (not temporary work)



If you cannot work this schedule or follow structured processes, please do not apply.



Skills We’re Looking For



Strong written and spoken English

Familiarity with Instagram, X, TikTok, Pinterest, and LinkedIn

Strong attention to detail and consistency

Ability to follow instructions precisely

Comfortable with repetitive but strategic execution

Prior social media or growth experience is a plus.

Professional, reliable, and accountable



Compensation & Benefits

Full training and onboarding provided

Opportunities for advancement within the company



About the Team



We are a fully remote team with members across different countries, including the Philippines.

Some team members have been with us for 6+ years, and we value long-term collaboration, consistency, and accountability.



How to Apply



If you are interested in this position and meet all the requirements above, please search for Ampfluence (#1 Instagram Growth Service) and reach out via email or our social media accounts.



Qualified candidates will be invited to a video interview.



We are actively hiring and plan to onboard at least 3 analysts as soon as possible.



Apply only if you are serious about becoming a long-term, high-performing member of our team.

title: B2B Cold Caller (Appointment Setter) - $150 per Sale
description:
* PLEASE READ THE ENTIRE DESCRIPTION BEFORE APPLYING *



Hey, I am JT! I am looking for 1-2 hard working appointment setters for my small business. I'm not a big call center where you are just a number. I'm a small business owner and looking for help doing outbound B2B cold calling for me. 



I help small businesses with an A.I. Receptionist to answer their missed calls and after-hours calls. This is a simple, in-demand offer. I just need help introducing it and getting business owners interested.



YOUR ROLE:

-- Source your own leads, leads are not provided.

-- Call 60-80 new contacts per 4-hour shift.

-- Offer them a free consultation to learn about A.I. 

-- If they show interest, schedule them for a consultation with me

-- That's it!  Once they show-up to their appointment, I take over.



REQUIREMENTS (DO NOT APPLY UNLESS YOU MEET ALL THE CRITERIA):

-- Experienced B2B Cold Calling and Appointment Setting. This means 100% cold calling — no prior contact, no warm leads.

-- If you have only worked inbound leads, warm leads, or marketing leads, DO NOT APPLY.

-- Clear and confident English communication with no heavy accent.

-- You are someone who is money hungry and motivated to hit your targets.

-- You are reliable with consistent attendance.



HOURS:

-- Part time (20 hours) to start, with the option for full time after 30 days.

-- Monday - Friday 10AM-2PM Pacific Standard Time (Tuesday - Saturday 1AM-5AM Philippine Time).



PAYMENT:

-- $4/hour base

-- $20 each qualified appointment that shows up

-- $150 each person who signs up for our services

-- Monthly bonuses



HOW TO APPLY:

-- Do not mention previous jobs that are not specifically related to cold calling B2B.

-- Put "Dial And Smile" as your subject line

-- Copy and paste the following questions and put answers in your application. Please use your own words, and do not use chat GPT or similar:



1) How many months/years of experience do you have specifically cold calling business to business and appointment setting (ONLY put jobs that were entirely cold leads....no warm leads, no inbound calls, etc):



2) What product/service were you offering and to who (please be detailed with this response and clear what you were offering):



3) How many calls per day did you average:



4) How many appointments per week did you average:



5) Did you source your own leads or were you given leads:



6) Do you currently have another job:



7) You call a business and a receptionist answers. They ask, “What is this regarding?”

What do you say to get through to the owner?

(Keep your answer to 1–2 sentences max and write it exactly how you would say it on a real call):



8) What makes you better than other cold callers applying for this role:

title: Structural Engineer (Remote – Singapore Projects)
description:
Job Summary

We are looking for a skilled Structural Engineer to support the design, analysis, and review of structural systems for projects primarily based in Singapore.



Portfolio is required — must showcase completed or contributed projects across residential, commercial, and industrial developments, including Singapore landed housing and/or high-rise projects



Key Responsibilities



-Design and analyze structural systems (reinforced concrete, steel, timber, and masonry) for new and existing structures

-Prepare structural calculations, technical reports, and detailed design documentation

-Review architectural and engineering drawings to ensure structural integrity and buildability

-Ensure compliance with Singapore building codes, standards, and regulatory requirements (e.g., BCA guidelines, Eurocodes with Singapore National Annex)

-Coordinate closely with architects, MEP consultants, contractors, and project managers in a remote setup

-Participate in design reviews, coordination meetings, and client discussions

-Provide technical support during construction, including responding to RFIs and design revisions

-Conduct structural assessments and propose strengthening or retrofitting solutions when required

-Manage multiple projects and deliver outputs within agreed timelines



Required Qualifications



-Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Civil or Structural Engineering

-Registered or eligible for Professional Engineer (PE) status (Singapore is an advantage)

-Minimum 3–8 years of relevant structural design experience

-Strong understanding of structural analysis and design principles

-Familiarity with Singapore codes and standards (BCA, Eurocodes, SS EN)

-Experience working with remote or distributed teams is preferred



Technical Skills



-Proficiency in structural analysis software such as ETABS, SAP2000, SAFE, STAAD, or similar

-Experience with BIM and drafting tools (AutoCAD, Revit, Tekla)

-Strong skills in structural calculations and technical documentation

-Ability to review and annotate drawings digitally

-Comfortable using collaboration tools such as Microsoft Teams, Zoom, and project management platforms



Soft Skills



-Strong communication skills (written and verbal)

-Excellent time management and ability to work independently

-Detail-oriented with strong problem-solving capabilities

-Self-motivated and able to thrive in a remote work environment



Work Environment & Benefits



-Opportunity to work on Singapore-based and international projects

-Exposure to multidisciplinary and multicultural teams

-Ongoing learning and professional development opportunities



Preferred (Nice to Have)



-Experience in Singapore landed housing or high-rise developments

-Knowledge of seismic design and advanced structural systems

-Familiarity with sustainable/green building practices (e.g., BCA Green Mark)

title: Creative Executor
description:
About DreamCare:

DreamCare is a Dutch DTC sleep brand. Our flagship product is an anti-snoring mouthpiece — the best-selling one in the Netherlands. We help people sleep better through simple, accessible and effective products. 

We run paid social ads (Meta) as our primary growth channel and produce a high volume of creatives every week. We work with a Dutch media agency that provides us with weekly creative briefings. Your job is to bring those briefings to life.



What you will be doing

You will produce ad creatives based on structured weekly briefings. Every briefing contains the persona, angle, hook, copy direction and CTA — your job is to execute.



Your weekly output will include:

• Video ads — D2C style (from a baseline video + brief, or from scratch based on a short creative input)

• Static ads (1:1 and 9:16 formats)

• Animated GIFs (1:1 and 9:16 formats)



Important: all briefings are written in English. We recommend working in English throughout — it makes the brief easier to understand and execute. Dutch translation is applied at the end using DeepL. You do not need to speak Dutch.



What we are looking for

• Strong skills in all three: static design, GIF animation and video editing

• Experience making ads for DTC or e-commerce brands (Meta, TikTok, YouTube)

• Able to follow a creative brief closely and execute without much back-and-forth

• Uses AI tools to improve copy quality and creative output

• Understands basic DTC ad formats: testimonial, problem-solution, before/after, USP-driven, objection-busting

• Understands concepts like hook, angle, CTA, and funnel stages (TOF/MOF/BOF)

• Reliable, fast turnaround, communicates clearly in English

• Portfolio showing ad creatives — not just social media posts or branding work



What we offer

• Hours: approximately 24 hours per week at $8/hour. Weekly hours may vary depending on the batch, but this is what we are working towards together — and it grows with the quality you deliver

• Consistent, ongoing work — this is not a one-off project

• Clear briefings every week — no guessing what to make

• Room to grow and take on more responsibility over time

• Direct communication with the founder



How to apply

Send a short intro and your best examples of ad creatives — statics, GIFs and/or video ads. Show us what you are most proud of. Based on your portfolio we will select candidates for a short paid trial assignment. No trial without seeing your work first.

Applications without creative examples will not be reviewed.

title: Bilingual Sales Closer (English + Spanish) | Concierge & Tours | Base + Commission
description:
Hiring Freelance Sales Closers — Premium Concierge & Tours

We're a concierge, tours, and premium services company catering to international hotel guests, and we're expanding our sales team. We need hungry closers ready to convert warm leads into paying clients.

What you'll do:

You don't need to find leads — we deliver them to you. Your job is to close:



Call and engage international guests interested in our services.

Send quotes and personalized estimates.

Cross-sell tours, experiences, and additional services.

Generate invoices and manage payment collection.

Track everything in our CRM until the deal is closed.

Proven sales experience, ideally in tourism, hospitality, or premium services, with real closing ability and a commercial mindset.

Fluent English (C1 minimum) AND Spanish — you must be able to communicate confidently with international clients in both languages, by call and message. This is non-negotiable.

Experience using CRMs like HubSpot, Pipedrive, Zoho, Go High Level, or similar (pipeline management and lead follow-up).

Basic Excel skills for sales tracking, reporting, and goal monitoring.

High energy, charisma, and a natural ability to connect with clients and inspire trust from the first interaction.



What we offer:



Base pay: competitive monthly rate (to be discussed based on experience).





Commission on every closed sale, the more you sell, the more you earn.





100% remote, freelance setup.

Warm leads delivered — you focus only on closing.

Premium industry with attractive ticket sizes to maximize your commission.



Selection process:

To apply, tell us:



Your sales experience (industry and concrete results — deals closed, conversion rates, etc.).

Your English AND Spanish level, and how you've used them professionally.

Which CRMs you've worked with.

Why you're the ideal closer for this role.



Generic applications will be discarded. We want to see your spark from the very first message.

title: Virtual Assistant for a house keeping company in San Francisco (Operations + Sales)
description:
Role Overview

We are looking for a self-starter who can handle the dual challenge of managing daily operations and driving sales growth. You will be responsible for keeping our cleaning schedule running smoothly while actively closing leads and converting customers into recurring clients. This is a stable, long-term position for someone who is coachable, adapts quickly, and doesn't need constant supervision to get the job done.



Key Responsibilities

Manage the Schedule: Handle daily cleaning appointments, coordinate the team, and fix last-minute gaps or call-outs.Drive Sales: Cold call to find new business, follow up with past clients, and convert one-time bookings into recurring accounts.Close Leads: Make outbound calls to leads and maintain a high closing rate to keep the calendar full.Handle Hiring: Recruit, screen, and help hire new cleaners to keep the team staffed.Customer Support: Manage all texts,
s, and phone calls while ensuring every job is documented and finished correctly.Keep Systems Organized: Maintain the CRM, update internal systems, and run daily task lists.Communicate: Flag issues quickly and stay proactive so nothing falls through the cracks.



Qualifications

Bilingual: Must be fluent in both English and Spanish.Sales Expert: Proven sales experience is non-negotiable. You must have a closing rate of 45%+ on calls.Service Minded: Previous experience in customer service and working in a startup or fast-paced environment.Tech Savvy: Comfortable using Google Workspace, CRMs, and scheduling tools.Independent: Strong organizational skills and the ability to make decisions on your own.Preferred: Background in timeshare sales, high-volume sales, dispatching, or service-based businesses.



Schedule & Requirements

Hours: Monday – Saturday (6 days/week), 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM (30-minute lunch).Availability: You must be responsive during working hours. No ghosting or inconsistency.Accountability: You must follow through on every assigned task and be comfortable handling both operations and sales.



Application Instructions

To apply, please provide your sales experience and closing rate, your customer service history, and any startup experience you have. List the tools/software you’ve used and confirm your availability and time zone. To prove you read the entire description, please include your favorite color in your response. Finally, let us know why you are looking for a long-term role.



Pay & Commission Structure

Base Pay: Starting at $40–$45 per day.Review Bonus: $3 per review generated.Recurring Bonus: $8 per converted recurring customer.Booking Bonus: $5 per closed booking added to the calendar.Hiring Bonus: $10 per successful hire (after 30-day retention with at least 5 jobs done in a month).

This Role Is NOT for You If:

You lack sales experience or cannot confidently close on calls.You are uncomfortable with outbound or cold calling.You need constant supervision or struggle with organization.You are looking for short-term or part-time work.



If you really meet the requirements, I leave you the contact
